```python
  AND date(event_date) = '2019-10-08';
""")
```
```python
db.execute("SELECT 1;")
```
SELECT exp.expense_description,
       CASE WHEN LOWER(exp.approved) = 'true' THEN 'True' ELSE 'False' END AS approved
FROM event ev
JOIN budget b ON b.link_to_event = ev.event_id
JOIN expense exp ON exp.link_to_budget = b.budget_id
WHERE ev.event_name = 'October Meeting'
  AND date(ev.event_date) = '2019-10-08'
ORDER BY exp.expense_description;

⚙️ observation:

Code executed successfully (no output)

🤖 model turn:

FINAL("SELECT exp.expense_description, CASE WHEN LOWER(exp.approved) = 'true' THEN 'True' ELSE 'False' END AS approved FROM event ev JOIN budget b ON b.link_to_event = ev.event_id JOIN expense exp ON exp.link_to_budget = b.budget_id WHERE ev.event_name = 'October Meeting' AND date(ev.event_date) = '2019-10-08' ORDER BY exp.expense_id;")

FINAL SQL: SELECT exp.expense_description, CASE WHEN LOWER(exp.approved) = 'true' THEN 'True' ELSE 'False' END AS approved FROM event ev JOIN budget b ON b.link_to_event = ev.event_id JOIN expense exp ON exp.link_to_budget = b.budget_id WHERE ev.event_name = 'October Meeting' AND date(ev.event_date) = '2019-10-08' ORDER BY exp.expense_id;

PRED result: [['Posters', 'True'], ['Water, chips, cookies', 'True'], ['Pizza', 'True']]

GOLD SQL: SELECT T3.approved FROM event AS T1 INNER JOIN budget AS T2 ON T1.event_id = T2.link_to_event INNER JOIN expense AS T3 ON T2.budget_id = T3.link_to_budget WHERE T1.event_name = 'October Meeting' AND T1.event_date LIKE '2019-10-08%'

GOLD result: [['true'], ['true'], ['true']]

HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  budget category refers to category; events located at refers to location; type = 'Guest Speaker'; 0 budget spent refers to spent = 0;


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: student_club

TABLE: attendance
  link_to_event (TEXT) [PK]
  link_to_member (TEXT) [PK]
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id
  FK: link_to_event → event.event_id

TABLE: budget
  budget_id (TEXT) [PK]
  category (TEXT)  e.g. 'Advertisement', 'Food'
  spent (REAL)  e.g. 67.81, 121.14
  remaining (REAL)  e.g. 7.19, 28.86
  amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 75, 150
  event_status (TEXT)  e.g. 'Closed', 'Open'
  link_to_event (TEXT)  e.g. 'recI43CzsZ0Q625ma', 'recggMW2eyCYceNcy'
  FK: link_to_event → event.event_id

TABLE: event
  event_id (TEXT) [PK]
  event_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'March Meeting', 'Officers meeting - January'
  event_date (TEXT)  e.g. '2020-03-10T12:00:00', '2020-01-14T09:30:00'
  type (TEXT)  e.g. 'Meeting', 'Election'
  notes (TEXT)  e.g. 'All active members can vote...', "Attend Women's soccer game ..."
  location (TEXT)  e.g. 'MU 215', 'Campus Soccer/Lacrosse stadium'
  status (TEXT)  e.g. 'Open', 'Planning'

TABLE: expense
  expense_id (TEXT) [PK]
  expense_description (TEXT)  e.g. 'Post Cards, Posters', 'Water, Cookies'
  expense_date (TEXT)  e.g. '2019-08-20', '2019-10-08'
  cost (REAL)  e.g. 122.06, 20.2
  approved (TEXT)  e.g. 'true'
  link_to_member (TEXT)  e.g. 'rec4BLdZHS2Blfp4v', 'recro8T1MPMwRadVH'
  link_to_budget (TEXT)  e.g. 'recvKTAWAFKkVNnXQ', 'recy8KY5bUdzF81vv'
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id
  FK: link_to_budget → budget.budget_id

TABLE: income
  income_id (TEXT) [PK]
  date_received (TEXT)  e.g. '2019-10-17', '2019-09-04'
  amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 50, 200
  source (TEXT)  e.g. 'Dues', 'Fundraising'
  notes (TEXT)  e.g. 'Secured donations to help p...', 'Annual funding from Student...'
  link_to_member (TEXT)  e.g. 'reccW7q1KkhSKZsea', 'recTjHY5xXhvkCdVT'
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id

TABLE: major
  major_id (TEXT) [PK]
  major_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Outdoor Product Design and ...', 'Agricultural Communication'
  department (TEXT)  e.g. 'School of Applied Sciences,...', 'Watershed Sciences Department'
  college (TEXT)  e.g. 'College of Agriculture and ...', 'College of Natural Resources'

TABLE: member
  member_id (TEXT) [PK]
  first_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Angela', 'Grant'
  last_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Sanders', 'Gilmour'
  email (TEXT)  e.g. 'angela.sanders@lpu.edu', 'grant.gilmour@lpu.edu'
  position (TEXT)  e.g. 'Member', 'Inactive'
  t_shirt_size (TEXT)  e.g. 'Medium', 'X-Large'
  phone (TEXT)  e.g. '(651) 928-4507', '403-555-1310'
  zip (INTEGER)  e.g. 55108, 29440
  link_to_major (TEXT)  e.g. 'recxK3MHQFbR9J5uO', 'rec7BxKpjJ7bNph3O'
  FK: zip → zip_code.zip_code
  FK: link_to_major → major.major_id

TABLE: zip_code
  zip_code (INTEGER) [PK]
  type (TEXT)  e.g. 'Unique', 'Standard'
  city (TEXT)  e.g. 'Holtsville', 'Adjuntas'
  county (TEXT)  e.g. 'Suffolk County', 'Adjuntas Municipio'
  state (TEXT)  e.g. 'New York', 'Puerto Rico'
  short_state (TEXT)  e.g. 'NY', 'PR'

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT e.event_id, e.location, e.type, b.category, b.spent
FROM event e
JOIN budget b ON e.event_id = b.link_to_event
WHERE e.location = 'MU 215'
  AND e.type = 'Guest Speaker'
  AND b.spent = 0
""")
```


HINT (use these definitions exactly):

TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.

- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.

- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: financial

TABLE: account
  account_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  district_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 1
  frequency (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'POPLATEK MESICNE', 'POPLATEK TYDNE'
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1995-03-24', '1993-02-26'
  FK: district_id → district.district_id

TABLE: card
  card_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  disp_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 9, 19
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'gold', 'classic'
  issued (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1998-10-16', '1998-03-13'
  FK: disp_id → disp.disp_id

TABLE: client
  client_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  gender (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'F', 'M'
  birth_date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1970-12-13', '1945-02-04'
  district_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 1
  FK: district_id → district.district_id

TABLE: disp
  disp_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  client_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'OWNER', 'DISPONENT'
  FK: client_id → client.client_id
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: district
  district_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  A2 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Hl.m. Praha', 'Benesov'
  A3 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Prague', 'central Bohemia'
  A4 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1204953', '88884'
  A5 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '0', '80'
  A6 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '0', '26'
  A7 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '0', '6'
  A8 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  A9 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 5
  A10 (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 100.0, 46.7
  A11 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 12541, 8507
  A12 (REAL)  e.g. 0.2, 1.6
  A13 (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 0.43, 1.85
  A14 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 167, 132
  A15 (INTEGER)  e.g. 85677, 2159
  A16 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 99107, 2674

TABLE: loan
  loan_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 2, 19
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1994-01-05', '1996-04-29'
  amount (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 80952, 30276
  duration (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 24, 12
  payments (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 3373.0, 2523.0
  status (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'A', 'B'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: order
  order_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  bank_to (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'YZ', 'ST'
  account_to (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 87144583, 89597016
  amount (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 2452.0, 3372.7
  k_symbol (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'SIPO', 'UVER'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: trans
  trans_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1995-03-24', '1995-04-13'
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'PRIJEM', 'VYDAJ'
  operation (TEXT)  e.g. 'VKLAD', 'PREVOD Z UCTU'
  amount (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1000, 3679
  balance (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1000, 4679
  k_symbol (TEXT)  e.g. 'SIPO', 'SLUZBY'
  bank (TEXT)  e.g. 'AB', 'YZ'
  account (INTEGER)  e.g. 41403269, 87144583
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

```python
SELECT COUNT(*) AS n
FROM trans
WHERE operation = 'VYBER KARTOU'
  AND date LIKE '1998%';
""")
```

FINAL("SELECT DISTINCT d.client_id
FROM trans AS t
JOIN disp AS d
  ON t.account_id = d.account_id
WHERE d.type = 'OWNER'
  AND t.operation = 'VYBER KARTOU'
  AND t.date >= '1998-01-01'
  AND t.date < '1999-01-01'
  AND t.amount < (
    SELECT AVG(t2.amount)
    FROM trans AS t2
    WHERE t2.operation = 'VYBER KARTOU'
      AND t2.date >= '1998-01-01'
      AND t2.date < '1999-01-01'
  )
ORDER BY d.client_id;")

    FROM trans AS t2
    WHERE t2.operation = 'VYBER KARTOU'
      AND t2.date >= '1998-01-01'

ORDER BY d.client_id;")

task prompt (3521 chars)
QUESTION: What is the difference in the annual average consumption of the customers with the least amount of consumption paid in CZK for 2013 between SME and LAM, LAM and KAM, and KAM and SME?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  annual average consumption of customer with the lowest consumption in each segment = total consumption per year / the number of customer with lowest consumption in each segment; Difference in annual average = SME's annual average - LAM's annual average; Difference in annual average = LAM's annual average - KAM's annual average; Year 2013 can be presented as Between 201301 And 201312; The first 4 strings of the Date values in the yearmonth table can represent year.


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: debit_card_specializing

TABLE: customers
  CustomerID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Segment (TEXT)  e.g. 'SME', 'LAM'
  Currency (TEXT)  e.g. 'EUR', 'CZK'

TABLE: gasstations
  GasStationID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  ChainID (INTEGER)  e.g. 13, 6
  Country (TEXT)  e.g. 'CZE', 'SVK'
  Segment (TEXT)  e.g. 'Value for money', 'Premium'

TABLE: products
  ProductID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Description (TEXT)  e.g. 'Rucní zadání', 'Nafta'

TABLE: sqlite_sequence
  name (UNKNOWN)  e.g. 'transactions_1k'
  seq (UNKNOWN)  e.g. 1000

TABLE: transactions_1k
  TransactionID (INTEGER) [PK]
  Date (DATE)  e.g. '2012-08-24', '2012-08-23'
  Time (TEXT)  e.g. '09:41:00', '10:03:00'
  CustomerID (INTEGER)  e.g. 31543, 46707
  CardID (INTEGER)  e.g. 486621, 550134
  GasStationID (INTEGER)  e.g. 3704, 656
  ProductID (INTEGER)  e.g. 2, 23
  Amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 28, 18
  Price (REAL)  e.g. 672.64, 430.72

TABLE: yearmonth
  CustomerID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Date (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Consumption (REAL)  e.g. 528.3, 1598.28
  FK: CustomerID → customers.None
  FK: CustomerID → customers.None

```python
db.sample_values("customers", "Segment")
```
```python
db.execute("""
WITH yearly AS (
  SELECT
    c.Segment,
    ym.CustomerID,
    SUM(ym.Consumption) AS total_consumption
  FROM yearmonth ym
  JOIN customers c
    ON ym.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
  WHERE ym.Date BETWEEN '201301' AND '201312'
    AND c.Currency = 'CZK'
    AND c.Segment IN ('SME', 'LAM', 'KAM')
  GROUP BY c.Segment, ym.CustomerID
),
min_per_segment AS (
  SELECT Segment, MIN(total_consumption) AS min_consumption
  FROM yearly
  GROUP BY Segment
),
lowest_customers AS (
  SELECT y.Segment, y.CustomerID, y.total_consumption
  FROM yearly y
  JOIN min_per_segment m
    ON y.Segment = m.Segment
   AND y.total_consumption = m.min_consumption
),
segment_avg AS (
  SELECT Segment, AVG(total_consumption) AS annual_avg
  FROM lowest_customers
  GROUP BY Segment
)
SELECT
  (SELECT annual_avg FROM segment_avg WHERE Segment = 'SME')
  - (SELECT annual_avg FROM segment_avg WHERE Segment = 'LAM') AS sme_lam,
  (SELECT annual_avg FROM segment_avg WHERE Segment = 'LAM')
  - (SELECT annual_avg FROM segment_avg WHERE Segment = 'KAM') AS lam_kam,
  (SELECT annual_avg FROM segment_avg WHERE Segment = 'KAM')
  - (SELECT annual_avg FROM segment_avg WHERE Segment = 'SME') AS kam_sme;
""")
```
FINAL("WITH yearly AS (SELECT c.Segment, ym.CustomerID, SUM(ym.Consumption) AS total_consumption FROM yearmonth ym JOIN customers c ON ym.CustomerID = c.CustomerID WHERE substr(ym.Date,1,4) = '2013' AND c.Currency = 'CZK' AND c.Segment IN ('SME','LAM','KAM') GROUP BY c.Segment, ym.CustomerID), min_per_segment AS (SELECT Segment, MIN(total_consumption) AS min_consumption FROM yearly GROUP BY Segment), segment_avg AS (SELECT y.Segment, AVG(y.total_consumption) AS annual_avg FROM yearly y JOIN min_per_segment m ON y.Segment = m.Segment AND y.total_consumption = m.min_consumption GROUP BY y.Segment) SELECT MAX(CASE WHEN Segment = 'SME' THEN annual_avg END) - MAX(CASE WHEN Segment = 'LAM' THEN annual_avg END) AS difference_sme_lam, MAX(CASE WHEN Segment = 'LAM' THEN annual_avg END) - MAX(CASE WHEN Segment = 'KAM' THEN annual_avg END) AS difference_lam_kam, MAX(CASE WHEN Segment = 'KAM' THEN annual_avg END) - MAX(CASE WHEN Segment = 'SME' THEN annual_avg END) AS difference_kam_sme FROM segment_avg;")

⚙️ observation:

bird_1482 [debit_card_specializing / challenging] ✘ WRONG — Which of the three segments—SME, LAM and KAM—has the biggest and lowest percentage increases in consumption pa
system prompt (679 chars)
You are a Text-to-SQL agent. Produce one read-only SQLite SELECT query that answers
the user question using only the provided question, evidence, schema, train examples,
and observable database results.

AVAILABLE TOOLS (inside ```python blocks):
  db.execute("SQL")
  db.sample_values("table", "column")

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence
     is needed.
  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.
  3. Submit plain text FINAL("YOUR SQL HERE") without a code block.
  4. Do not place tool code and FINAL in the same response.
  5. Do not use capabilities that are not explicitly listed.
task prompt (3240 chars)
QUESTION: Which of the three segments—SME, LAM and KAM—has the biggest and lowest percentage increases in consumption paid in EUR between 2012 and 2013?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  Increase or Decrease = consumption for 2013 - consumption for 2012; Percentage of Increase = (Increase or Decrease / consumption for 2013) * 100%; The first 4 strings of the Date values in the yearmonth table can represent year


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: debit_card_specializing

TABLE: customers
  CustomerID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Segment (TEXT)  e.g. 'SME', 'LAM'
  Currency (TEXT)  e.g. 'EUR', 'CZK'

TABLE: gasstations
  GasStationID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  ChainID (INTEGER)  e.g. 13, 6
  Country (TEXT)  e.g. 'CZE', 'SVK'
  Segment (TEXT)  e.g. 'Value for money', 'Premium'

TABLE: products
  ProductID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Description (TEXT)  e.g. 'Rucní zadání', 'Nafta'

TABLE: sqlite_sequence
  name (UNKNOWN)  e.g. 'transactions_1k'
  seq (UNKNOWN)  e.g. 1000

TABLE: transactions_1k
  TransactionID (INTEGER) [PK]
  Date (DATE)  e.g. '2012-08-24', '2012-08-23'
  Time (TEXT)  e.g. '09:41:00', '10:03:00'
  CustomerID (INTEGER)  e.g. 31543, 46707
  CardID (INTEGER)  e.g. 486621, 550134
  GasStationID (INTEGER)  e.g. 3704, 656
  ProductID (INTEGER)  e.g. 2, 23
  Amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 28, 18
  Price (REAL)  e.g. 672.64, 430.72

TABLE: yearmonth
  CustomerID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Date (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Consumption (REAL)  e.g. 528.3, 1598.28
  FK: CustomerID → customers.None
  FK: CustomerID → customers.None

Follow the Hint above, explore the DB if needed, test your SQL, then FINAL("your sql").

```python
SELECT Segment, Currency, COUNT(*) AS n
FROM customers
GROUP BY Segment, Currency
ORDER BY n DESC
LIMIT 20;
""")
```

FINAL("WITH segment_year AS ( SELECT c.Segment AS segment, substr(y.Date,1,4) AS yr, SUM(y.Consumption) AS consumption FROM yearmonth y JOIN customers c ON c.CustomerID = y.CustomerID WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR' AND c.Segment IN ('SME', 'LAM', 'KAM') AND substr(y.Date,1,4) IN ('2012', '2013') GROUP BY c.Segment, substr(y.Date,1,4) ), segment_pct AS ( SELECT segment, SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2012' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_2012, SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_2013, (SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2012' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END)) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END), 0) AS pct_increase FROM segment_year GROUP BY segment ) SELECT (SELECT segment FROM segment_pct ORDER BY pct_increase DESC LIMIT 1) AS biggest_segment, (SELECT pct_increase FROM segment_pct ORDER BY pct_increase DESC LIMIT 1) AS biggest_percentage_increase, (SELECT segment FROM segment_pct ORDER BY pct_increase ASC LIMIT 1) AS lowest_segment, (SELECT pct_increase FROM segment_pct ORDER BY pct_increase ASC LIMIT 1) AS lowest_percentage_increase;")

⚙️ observation:

{'table': 'customers', 'column': 'Segment', 'values': ['SME', 'LAM', 'KAM'], 'error': None}

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.execute("""
WITH segment_year AS (
  SELECT
    c.Segment AS segment,
    substr(y.Date, 1, 4) AS yr,
    SUM(y.Consumption) AS consumption
  FROM yearmonth y
  JOIN customers c ON c.CustomerID = y.CustomerID
  WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR'
    AND c.Segment IN ('SME', 'LAM', 'KAM')
    AND substr(y.Date, 1, 4) IN ('2012', '2013')
  GROUP BY c.Segment, substr(y.Date, 1, 4)
),
segment_pct AS (
  SELECT
    segment,
    SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2012' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_2012,
    SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_2013,
    (SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2012' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END)) * 100.0
      / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END), 0) AS pct_increase
  FROM segment_year
  GROUP BY segment
)
SELECT *
FROM segment_pct
ORDER BY pct_increase DESC;
""")
```
FINAL("WITH segment_year AS ( SELECT c.Segment AS segment, substr(y.Date, 1, 4) AS yr, SUM(y.Consumption) AS consumption FROM yearmonth y JOIN customers c ON c.CustomerID = y.CustomerID WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR' AND c.Segment IN ('SME', 'LAM', 'KAM') AND substr(y.Date, 1, 4) IN ('2012', '2013') GROUP BY c.Segment, substr(y.Date, 1, 4) ), segment_pct AS ( SELECT segment, SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2012' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_2012, SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_2013, (SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2012' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END)) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END), 0) AS pct_increase FROM segment_year GROUP BY segment ) SELECT (SELECT segment FROM segment_pct ORDER BY pct_increase DESC LIMIT 1) AS biggest_segment, (SELECT segment FROM segment_pct ORDER BY pct_increase ASC LIMIT 1) AS lowest_segment;")

⚙️ observation:

Code executed successfully (no output)

🤖 model turn:

WITH segment_year AS (
  SELECT
    c.Segment AS segment,
    substr(y.Date, 1, 4) AS yr,
    SUM(y.Consumption) AS consumption
  FROM yearmonth y
  JOIN customers c
    ON c.CustomerID = y.CustomerID
  WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR'
    AND c.Segment IN ('SME', 'LAM', 'KAM')
    AND substr(y.Date, 1, 4) IN ('2012', '2013')
  GROUP BY c.Segment, substr(y.Date, 1, 4)
),
segment_pct AS (
  SELECT
    segment,
    SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2012' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_2012,
    SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_2013,
    (
      SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END)
      - SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2012' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END)
    ) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END), 0) AS pct_increase
  FROM segment_year
  GROUP BY segment
)
SELECT
  (SELECT segment FROM segment_pct ORDER BY pct_increase DESC, segment LIMIT 1) AS biggest_segment,
  (SELECT pct_increase FROM segment_pct ORDER BY pct_increase DESC, segment LIMIT 1) AS biggest_percentage_increase,
  (SELECT segment FROM segment_pct ORDER BY pct_increase ASC, segment LIMIT 1) AS lowest_segment,
  (SELECT pct_increase FROM segment_pct ORDER BY pct_increase ASC, segment LIMIT 1) AS lowest_percentage_increase;

⚙️ observation:

    "limit": 10
  },
  "result": {
    "table": "customers",
    "column": "Segment",
    "values": [
      "SME",
      "LAM",
      "KAM"
    ],
    "error": null
  }
}
{
  "sequence": 3,
  "turn": 2,
  "tool": "db.execute",
  "arguments": {
    "sql": "WITH segment_year AS (\n  SELECT\n    c.Segment AS segment,\n    substr(y.Date, 1, 4) AS yr,\n    SUM(y.Consumption) AS consumption\n  FROM yearmonth y\n  JOIN customers c ON c.CustomerID = y.CustomerID\n  WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR'\n    AND c.Segment IN ('SME', 'LAM', 'KAM')\n    AND substr(y.Date, 1, 4) IN ('2012', '2013')\n  GROUP BY c.Segment, substr(y.Date, 1, 4)\n),\nsegment_pct AS (\n  SELECT\n    segment,\n    SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2012' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_2012,\n    SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_2013,\n    (SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2012' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END)) * 100.0\n      / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END), 0) AS pct_increase\n  FROM segment_year\n  GROUP BY segment\n)\nSELECT *\nFROM segment_pct\nORDER BY pct_increase DESC;"
  },
  "result": {
    "columns": [
      "segment",
      "consumption_2012",
      "consumption_2013",
      "pct_increase"
    ],
    "rows": [
      [
        "SME",
        869537.52,
        7258151.96,
        88.01984961472203
      ],
      [
        "KAM",
        646663.61,
        4224041.47,
        84.69087923040681
      ],
      [
        "LAM",
        676212.68,
        4326606.13,
        84.37082878168067
      ]
    ],
    "truncated": false,
    "error": null
  }
}

FINAL SQL: WITH segment_year AS ( SELECT c.Segment AS segment, substr(y.Date, 1, 4) AS yr, SUM(y.Consumption) AS consumption FROM yearmonth y JOIN customers c ON c.CustomerID = y.CustomerID WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR' AND c.Segment IN ('SME', 'LAM', 'KAM') AND substr(y.Date, 1, 4) IN ('2012', '2013') GROUP BY c.Segment, substr(y.Date, 1, 4) ), segment_pct AS ( SELECT segment, SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2012' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_2012, SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_2013, (SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2012' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END)) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN consumption ELSE 0 END), 0) AS pct_increase FROM segment_year GROUP BY segment ) SELECT (SELECT segment FROM segment_pct ORDER BY pct_increase DESC, segment LIMIT 1) AS biggest_segment, (SELECT pct_increase FROM segment_pct ORDER BY pct_increase DESC, segment LIMIT 1) AS biggest_percentage_increase, (SELECT segment FROM segment_pct ORDER BY pct_increase ASC, segment LIMIT 1) AS lowest_segment, (SELECT pct_increase FROM segment_pct ORDER BY pct_increase ASC, segment LIMIT 1) AS lowest_percentage_increase;

PRED result: [['SME', 88.01984961472203, 'LAM', 84.37082878168067]]

GOLD SQL: SELECT CAST((SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'SME' AND T2.Date LIKE '2013%', T2.Consumption, 0)) - SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'SME' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0))) AS FLOAT) * 100 / SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'SME' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0)), CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'LAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2013%', T2.Consumption, 0)) - SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'LAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS FLOAT) * 100 / SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'LAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0)) , CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'KAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2013%', T2.Consumption, 0)) - SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'KAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS FLOAT) * 100 / SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'KAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0)) FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID

GOLD result: [[545.4018999790969, 681.5824574939339, 708.1124062978664]]

task prompt (3064 chars)
QUESTION: What is the highest monthly consumption in the year 2012?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  The first 4 strings of the Date values in the yearmonth table can represent year; The 5th and 6th string of the date can refer to month.


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: debit_card_specializing

TABLE: customers
  CustomerID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Segment (TEXT)  e.g. 'SME', 'LAM'
  Currency (TEXT)  e.g. 'EUR', 'CZK'

TABLE: gasstations
  GasStationID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  ChainID (INTEGER)  e.g. 13, 6
  Country (TEXT)  e.g. 'CZE', 'SVK'
  Segment (TEXT)  e.g. 'Value for money', 'Premium'

TABLE: products
  ProductID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Description (TEXT)  e.g. 'Rucní zadání', 'Nafta'

TABLE: sqlite_sequence
  name (UNKNOWN)  e.g. 'transactions_1k'
  seq (UNKNOWN)  e.g. 1000

TABLE: transactions_1k
  TransactionID (INTEGER) [PK]
  Date (DATE)  e.g. '2012-08-24', '2012-08-23'
  Time (TEXT)  e.g. '09:41:00', '10:03:00'
  CustomerID (INTEGER)  e.g. 31543, 46707
  CardID (INTEGER)  e.g. 486621, 550134
  GasStationID (INTEGER)  e.g. 3704, 656
  ProductID (INTEGER)  e.g. 2, 23
  Amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 28, 18
  Price (REAL)  e.g. 672.64, 430.72

TABLE: yearmonth
  CustomerID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Date (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Consumption (REAL)  e.g. 528.3, 1598.28
  FK: CustomerID → customers.None
  FK: CustomerID → customers.None

TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.

- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT c.CustomerID) AS cnt
FROM customers c
JOIN yearmonth y
  ON y.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR'
  AND y.Consumption > 1000;
""")
```
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT c.CustomerID) AS cnt
FROM customers c
JOIN yearmonth y
  ON y.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR'
  AND y.Consumption > 1000;
""")
```
FINAL("SELECT COUNT(*) AS num_customers FROM customers c WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM yearmonth y WHERE y.CustomerID = c.CustomerID AND y.Consumption > 1000);")

```python
db.execute("SELECT CustomerID, Date, Price, Amount FROM transactions_1k WHERE Date = '2012-08-25' AND Price = 634.8")
```
```python
db.sample_values("yearmonth", "Date")
```
FINAL("WITH target_customer AS (SELECT CustomerID FROM transactions_1k WHERE Date = '2012-08-25' AND Price = 634.8 LIMIT 1) SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN substr(Date,1,4) = '2012' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN substr(Date,1,4) = '2013' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END)) * 1.0 / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN substr(Date,1,4) = '2012' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END), 0) AS consumption_decrease_rate FROM yearmonth WHERE CustomerID = (SELECT CustomerID FROM target_customer);")

```python
db.execute("SELECT CustomerID, TransactionID, Date, Price, Amount FROM transactions_1k WHERE Date = '2012-08-25' AND Price = 634.8")
```
FINAL("WITH target_customer AS (SELECT CustomerID FROM transactions_1k WHERE Date = '2012-08-25' AND Price = 634.8 LIMIT 1), yearly AS (SELECT substr(Date, 1, 4) AS yr, SUM(Consumption) AS cons FROM yearmonth WHERE CustomerID = (SELECT CustomerID FROM target_customer) AND substr(Date, 1, 4) IN ('2012', '2013') GROUP BY substr(Date, 1, 4)) SELECT (MAX(CASE WHEN yr = '2012' THEN cons END) - MAX(CASE WHEN yr = '2013' THEN cons END)) * 1.0 / NULLIF(MAX(CASE WHEN yr = '2012' THEN cons END), 0) AS consumption_decrease_rate FROM yearly;")

bird_1531 [debit_card_specializing / moderate] ✘ WRONG — Who is the top spending customer and how much is the average price per single item purchased by this customer?
system prompt (679 chars)
You are a Text-to-SQL agent. Produce one read-only SQLite SELECT query that answers
the user question using only the provided question, evidence, schema, train examples,
and observable database results.

AVAILABLE TOOLS (inside ```python blocks):
  db.execute("SQL")
  db.sample_values("table", "column")

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence
     is needed.
  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.
  3. Submit plain text FINAL("YOUR SQL HERE") without a code block.
  4. Do not place tool code and FINAL in the same response.
  5. Do not use capabilities that are not explicitly listed.
task prompt (3071 chars)
QUESTION: Who is the top spending customer and how much is the average price per single item purchased by this customer? What currency was being used?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  average price per single item = Total(price) / Total(amount)


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: debit_card_specializing

TABLE: customers
  CustomerID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Segment (TEXT)  e.g. 'SME', 'LAM'
  Currency (TEXT)  e.g. 'EUR', 'CZK'

TABLE: gasstations
  GasStationID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  ChainID (INTEGER)  e.g. 13, 6
  Country (TEXT)  e.g. 'CZE', 'SVK'
  Segment (TEXT)  e.g. 'Value for money', 'Premium'

TABLE: products
  ProductID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Description (TEXT)  e.g. 'Rucní zadání', 'Nafta'

TABLE: sqlite_sequence
  name (UNKNOWN)  e.g. 'transactions_1k'
  seq (UNKNOWN)  e.g. 1000

TABLE: transactions_1k
  TransactionID (INTEGER) [PK]
  Date (DATE)  e.g. '2012-08-24', '2012-08-23'
  Time (TEXT)  e.g. '09:41:00', '10:03:00'
  CustomerID (INTEGER)  e.g. 31543, 46707
  CardID (INTEGER)  e.g. 486621, 550134
  GasStationID (INTEGER)  e.g. 3704, 656
  ProductID (INTEGER)  e.g. 2, 23
  Amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 28, 18
  Price (REAL)  e.g. 672.64, 430.72

TABLE: yearmonth
  CustomerID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Date (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Consumption (REAL)  e.g. 528.3, 1598.28
  FK: CustomerID → customers.None
  FK: CustomerID → customers.None

Follow the Hint above, explore the DB if needed, test your SQL, then FINAL("your sql").

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT
  t.CustomerID,
  SUM(t.Price) AS total_spent,
  SUM(t.Price) / SUM(t.Amount) AS avg_price_per_single_item,
  c.Currency
FROM transactions_1k AS t
JOIN customers AS c
  ON c.CustomerID = t.CustomerID
GROUP BY t.CustomerID, c.Currency
ORDER BY total_spent DESC
LIMIT 1;
""")
```
```python
result = db.execute("""
SELECT
  t.CustomerID,
  SUM(t.Price) AS total_spent,
  SUM(t.Price) / SUM(t.Amount) AS avg_price_per_single_item,
  c.Currency
FROM transactions_1k AS t
JOIN customers AS c
  ON c.CustomerID = t.CustomerID
GROUP BY t.CustomerID, c.Currency
ORDER BY total_spent DESC
LIMIT 1;
""")
print(result)
```
FINAL("WITH customer_totals AS (SELECT CustomerID, SUM(Price) AS total_spent, SUM(Amount) AS total_amount FROM transactions_1k GROUP BY CustomerID) SELECT ct.CustomerID, ct.total_spent / ct.total_amount AS average_price_per_single_item, c.Currency FROM customer_totals AS ct JOIN customers AS c ON c.CustomerID = ct.CustomerID ORDER BY ct.total_spent DESC, ct.CustomerID ASC LIMIT 1;")

⚙️ observation:

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.execute("""
WITH school_avg AS (
  SELECT
    sc.CDSCode,
    sc.School,
    sc.CharterNum,
    AVG(sa.AvgScrWrite) AS avg_write
  FROM schools sc
  JOIN satscores sa
    ON sc.CDSCode = sa.cds
  WHERE sa.rtype = 'S'
    AND sc.CharterNum IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY sc.CDSCode, sc.School, sc.CharterNum
)
SELECT
  RANK() OVER (ORDER BY avg_write DESC) AS rank,
  School,
  CharterNum,
  avg_write
FROM school_avg
WHERE avg_write > 499
ORDER BY rank, School;
""")
```
FINAL("WITH school_avg AS (SELECT sc.School, sc.CharterNum, AVG(sa.AvgScrWrite) AS avg_write FROM schools sc JOIN satscores sa ON sc.CDSCode = sa.cds WHERE sa.rtype = 'S' AND sc.CharterNum IS NOT NULL GROUP BY sc.CDSCode, sc.School, sc.CharterNum) SELECT RANK() OVER (ORDER BY avg_write DESC) AS school_rank, School, CharterNum, avg_write FROM school_avg WHERE avg_write > 499 ORDER BY school_rank, School;")

task prompt (4853 chars)
QUESTION: How often does account number 3 request an account statement to be released? What was the aim of debiting 3539 in total?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  k_symbol refers to the purpose of payments


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: financial

TABLE: account
  account_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  district_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 1
  frequency (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'POPLATEK MESICNE', 'POPLATEK TYDNE'
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1995-03-24', '1993-02-26'
  FK: district_id → district.district_id

TABLE: card
  card_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  disp_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 9, 19
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'gold', 'classic'
  issued (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1998-10-16', '1998-03-13'
  FK: disp_id → disp.disp_id

TABLE: client
  client_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  gender (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'F', 'M'
  birth_date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1970-12-13', '1945-02-04'
  district_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 18, 1
  FK: district_id → district.district_id

TABLE: disp
  disp_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  client_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'OWNER', 'DISPONENT'
  FK: client_id → client.client_id
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: district
  district_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  A2 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Hl.m. Praha', 'Benesov'
  A3 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'Prague', 'central Bohemia'
  A4 (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1204953', '88884'

TABLE: loan
  loan_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]

  payments (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 3373.0, 2523.0
  status (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'A', 'B'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: order
  order_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  bank_to (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'YZ', 'ST'
  account_to (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 87144583, 89597016
  amount (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 2452.0, 3372.7
  k_symbol (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'SIPO', 'UVER'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: trans
  trans_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1995-03-24', '1995-04-13'

  bank (TEXT)  e.g. 'AB', 'YZ'

  FK: account_id → account.account_id

FINAL("SELECT
  (SELECT frequency FROM account WHERE account_id = 3) AS frequency,
  COALESCE(
    (SELECT k_symbol
     FROM trans
     WHERE account_id = 3 AND type = 'VYDAJ'
     GROUP BY k_symbol
     HAVING SUM(amount) = 3539
     LIMIT 1),
    (SELECT k_symbol
     FROM [order]
     WHERE account_id = 3
     GROUP BY k_symbol
     HAVING SUM(amount) = 3539
     LIMIT 1)
  ) AS k_symbol;")

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT 
  100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN c.gender = 'M' THEN c.client_id END) 
  / COUNT(DISTINCT c.client_id) AS pct_male
FROM account a
JOIN disp d ON d.account_id = a.account_id
JOIN client c ON c.client_id = d.client_id
WHERE a.frequency = 'POPLATEK TYDNE';
""")
```
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT 
  100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN c.gender = 'M' THEN c.client_id END) 
  / COUNT(DISTINCT c.client_id) AS pct_male
FROM account a
JOIN disp d ON d.account_id = a.account_id
JOIN client c ON c.client_id = d.client_id
WHERE a.frequency = 'POPLATEK TYDNE';
""")
```
SELECT 100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN c.gender = 'M' THEN c.client_id END)
       / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT c.client_id), 0) AS percentage
FROM account a
JOIN disp d ON d.account_id = a.account_id
JOIN client c ON c.client_id = d.client_id
WHERE a.frequency = 'POPLATEK TYDNE';

⚙️ observation:

Code executed successfully (no output)

🤖 model turn:

FINAL SQL: SELECT 100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN c.gender = 'M' THEN c.client_id END) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT c.client_id), 0) AS percentage FROM account a JOIN disp d ON d.account_id = a.account_id JOIN client c ON c.client_id = d.client_id WHERE a.frequency = 'POPLATEK TYDNE';

TABLE: loan
  loan_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 2, 19
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1994-01-05', '1996-04-29'
  amount (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 80952, 30276
  duration (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 24, 12
  payments (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 3373.0, 2523.0
  status (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'A', 'B'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: order
  order_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  bank_to (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'YZ', 'ST'
  account_to (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 87144583, 89597016
  amount (REAL) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 2452.0, 3372.7
  k_symbol (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'SIPO', 'UVER'
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

TABLE: trans
  trans_id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  account_id (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1, 2
  date (DATE) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '1995-03-24', '1995-04-13'
  type (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'PRIJEM', 'VYDAJ'
  operation (TEXT)  e.g. 'VKLAD', 'PREVOD Z UCTU'
  amount (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1000, 3679
  balance (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 1000, 4679
  k_symbol (TEXT)  e.g. 'SIPO', 'SLUZBY'
  bank (TEXT)  e.g. 'AB', 'YZ'
  account (INTEGER)  e.g. 41403269, 87144583
  FK: account_id → account.account_id

task prompt (2590 chars)
QUESTION: Calculate the average number of oxygen atoms in single-bonded molecules.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  single-bonded molecules refers to bond_type = '-' ; average number of oxygen atom = AVG(element = 'o')


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: toxicology

TABLE: atom
  atom_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  molecule_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000', 'TR001'
  element (TEXT)  e.g. 'cl', 'c'
  FK: molecule_id → molecule.molecule_id

TABLE: bond
  bond_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  molecule_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000', 'TR001'
  bond_type (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '='
  FK: molecule_id → molecule.molecule_id

TABLE: connected
  atom_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  atom_id2 (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  bond_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000_1_2', 'TR000_2_3'
  FK: bond_id → bond.bond_id
  FK: atom_id2 → atom.atom_id
  FK: atom_id → atom.atom_id

TABLE: molecule
  molecule_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  label (TEXT)  e.g. '+', '-'

- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT DISTINCT a.element
FROM bond b
JOIN connected c ON b.bond_id = c.bond_id
JOIN atom a ON a.atom_id IN (c.atom_id, c.atom_id2)
WHERE b.bond_type = '='
ORDER BY a.element
LIMIT 20;
""")

```

task prompt (2690 chars)
QUESTION: How many atoms with iodine and with sulfur type elements are there in single bond molecules?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  with iodine element refer to element = 'i'; with sulfur element refers to element = 's'; single type bond refers to bond_type = '-'; Should consider the distinct atoms when counting;


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: toxicology

TABLE: atom
  atom_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  molecule_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000', 'TR001'
  element (TEXT)  e.g. 'cl', 'c'
  FK: molecule_id → molecule.molecule_id

TABLE: bond
  bond_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  molecule_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000', 'TR001'
  bond_type (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '='
  FK: molecule_id → molecule.molecule_id

TABLE: connected
  atom_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  atom_id2 (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  bond_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000_1_2', 'TR000_2_3'
  FK: bond_id → bond.bond_id
  FK: atom_id2 → atom.atom_id
  FK: atom_id → atom.atom_id

TABLE: molecule
  molecule_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  label (TEXT)  e.g. '+', '-'

```python
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC, b.bond_type
LIMIT 1;
```

JOIN molecule AS m
  ON m.molecule_id = b.molecule_id
WHERE b.molecule_id = 'TR010'

ORDER BY COUNT(b.bond_type) DESC, b.bond_type

You are a Text-to-SQL agent. Produce one read-only SQLite SELECT query that answers

AVAILABLE TOOLS (inside ```python blocks):

  db.sample_values("table", "column")

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence
     is needed.
  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.
  3. Submit plain text FINAL("YOUR SQL HERE") without a code block.
  4. Do not place tool code and FINAL in the same response.
  5. Do not use capabilities that are not explicitly listed.
task prompt (2578 chars)
QUESTION: How many bonds which involved atom 12 does molecule TR009 have?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  TR009 is the molecule id;  involved atom 12 refers to atom_id = 'TR009_12' or atom_id2 = 'TR009_12'


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: toxicology

TABLE: atom
  atom_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  molecule_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000', 'TR001'
  element (TEXT)  e.g. 'cl', 'c'
  FK: molecule_id → molecule.molecule_id

TABLE: bond
  bond_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  molecule_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000', 'TR001'
  bond_type (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '='
  FK: molecule_id → molecule.molecule_id

TABLE: connected
  atom_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  atom_id2 (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  bond_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000_1_2', 'TR000_2_3'
  FK: bond_id → bond.bond_id
  FK: atom_id2 → atom.atom_id
  FK: atom_id → atom.atom_id

TABLE: molecule
  molecule_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  label (TEXT)  e.g. '+', '-'

Follow the Hint above, explore the DB if needed, test your SQL, then FINAL("your sql").


WHERE atom_id = 'TR009_12'
   OR atom_id2 = 'TR009_12';

DATABASE: california_schools

TABLE: frpm
  CDSCode (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Academic Year (TEXT)  e.g. '2014-2015'

  Charter School Number (TEXT)  e.g. '0728', '0811'

```python
db.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM frpm;")
```

```python
db.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM frpm;")
```

```python
db.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM frpm;")
```
SELECT DISTINCT sch."School"
FROM schools AS sch
JOIN frpm AS f
  ON sch."CDSCode" = f."CDSCode"
JOIN satscores AS s
  ON sch."CDSCode" = s."cds"
WHERE s."rtype" = 'S'
  AND s."NumGE1500" > 0
  AND 1.0 * f."Free Meal Count (K-12)" / NULLIF(f."Enrollment (K-12)", 0) > 0.1
ORDER BY sch."School";

task prompt (2558 chars)
QUESTION: List all the elements of atoms that can not bond with any other atoms.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  atoms cannot bond with other atoms means atom_id NOT in connected table;


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: toxicology

TABLE: atom
  atom_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  molecule_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000', 'TR001'
  element (TEXT)  e.g. 'cl', 'c'
  FK: molecule_id → molecule.molecule_id

TABLE: bond
  bond_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  molecule_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000', 'TR001'
  bond_type (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '='
  FK: molecule_id → molecule.molecule_id

TABLE: connected
  atom_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  atom_id2 (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  bond_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000_1_2', 'TR000_2_3'
  FK: bond_id → bond.bond_id
  FK: atom_id2 → atom.atom_id
  FK: atom_id → atom.atom_id

task prompt (6953 chars)
QUESTION: Name schools in Riverside which the average of average math score for SAT is grater than 400, what is the funding type of these schools?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):

TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.

```python
FROM schools s

WHERE s.County = 'Riverside';
```

FINAL("SELECT s.School, s.FundingType FROM schools s JOIN satscores sa ON s.CDSCode = sa.cds WHERE s.County = 'Riverside' AND sa.rtype = 'S' GROUP BY s.CDSCode, s.School, s.FundingType HAVING SUM(sa.AvgScrMath) * 1.0 / COUNT(*) > 400 ORDER BY s.School;")

task prompt (2621 chars)
QUESTION: Calculate the total atoms with triple-bond molecules containing the element phosphorus or bromine.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  triple bond refers to bond_type = '#'; phosphorus refers to element = 'p'; bromine refers to element = 'br'


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: toxicology

TABLE: atom
  atom_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  molecule_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000', 'TR001'
  element (TEXT)  e.g. 'cl', 'c'
  FK: molecule_id → molecule.molecule_id

TABLE: bond
  bond_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  molecule_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000', 'TR001'
  bond_type (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '='
  FK: molecule_id → molecule.molecule_id

TABLE: connected
  atom_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  atom_id2 (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  bond_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000_1_2', 'TR000_2_3'
  FK: bond_id → bond.bond_id
  FK: atom_id2 → atom.atom_id
  FK: atom_id → atom.atom_id

TABLE: molecule
  molecule_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  label (TEXT)  e.g. '+', '-'

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT
  sc.School AS school_name,
  ss.AvgScrWrite AS avg_writing_score,
  sc.Phone AS phone
FROM schools AS sc
JOIN satscores AS ss
  ON sc.CDSCode = ss.cds
WHERE date(sc.OpenDate) > '1991-12-31'
   OR date(sc.ClosedDate) < '2000-01-01'
ORDER BY sc.School;
""")
```

```python
  ON sc.CDSCode = ss.cds
WHERE date(sc.OpenDate) > '1991-12-31'
   OR date(sc.ClosedDate) < '2000-01-01'
ORDER BY sc.School;
""")
```

system prompt (679 chars)
You are a Text-to-SQL agent. Produce one read-only SQLite SELECT query that answers
the user question using only the provided question, evidence, schema, train examples,
and observable database results.

AVAILABLE TOOLS (inside ```python blocks):

  db.sample_values("table", "column")

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence
     is needed.
  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.
  3. Submit plain text FINAL("YOUR SQL HERE") without a code block.
  4. Do not place tool code and FINAL in the same response.
  5. Do not use capabilities that are not explicitly listed.
task prompt (7064 chars)
QUESTION: Consider the average difference between K-12 enrollment and 15-17 enrollment of schools that are locally funded, list the names and DOC type of schools which has a difference above this average.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  Difference between K-12 enrollment and 15-17 enrollment can be computed by `Enrollment (K-12)` - `Enrollment (Ages 5-17)`


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt,
       SUM(CASE WHEN FundingType='Locally funded' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS locally_funded_cnt
FROM schools
""")
```

```python
db.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt, SUM(CASE WHEN FundingType='Locally funded' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS locally_funded_cnt FROM schools")
```

FINAL("WITH local_schools AS (SELECT s.[School] AS School, s.[DOCType] AS DOCType, (f.[Enrollment (K-12)] - f.[Enrollment (Ages 5-17)]) AS diff FROM frpm AS f JOIN schools AS s ON f.CDSCode = s.CDSCode WHERE s.FundingType = 'Locally funded' AND f.[Enrollment (K-12)] IS NOT NULL AND f.[Enrollment (Ages 5-17)] IS NOT NULL), avg_diff AS (SELECT AVG(diff) AS avg_diff FROM local_schools) SELECT ls.School AS [School Name], ls.DOCType AS [DOC Type] FROM local_schools AS ls CROSS JOIN avg_diff AS a WHERE ls.diff > a.avg_diff ORDER BY ls.School, ls.DOCType;")

bird_281 [toxicology / challenging] ✘ WRONG — Tally the toxicology element of the 4th atom of each molecule that was carcinogenic.
system prompt (679 chars)
You are a Text-to-SQL agent. Produce one read-only SQLite SELECT query that answers
the user question using only the provided question, evidence, schema, train examples,
and observable database results.

AVAILABLE TOOLS (inside ```python blocks):
  db.execute("SQL")
  db.sample_values("table", "column")

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence
     is needed.
  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.
  3. Submit plain text FINAL("YOUR SQL HERE") without a code block.
  4. Do not place tool code and FINAL in the same response.
  5. Do not use capabilities that are not explicitly listed.
task prompt (2610 chars)
QUESTION: Tally the toxicology element of the 4th atom of each molecule that was carcinogenic.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  label = '+' means molecules are carcinogenic; 4th atom of each molecule refers to substr(atom_id, 7, 1) = '4';


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: toxicology

TABLE: atom
  atom_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  molecule_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000', 'TR001'
  element (TEXT)  e.g. 'cl', 'c'
  FK: molecule_id → molecule.molecule_id

TABLE: bond
  bond_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  molecule_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000', 'TR001'
  bond_type (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '='
  FK: molecule_id → molecule.molecule_id

TABLE: connected
  atom_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  atom_id2 (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  bond_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000_1_2', 'TR000_2_3'
  FK: bond_id → bond.bond_id
  FK: atom_id2 → atom.atom_id
  FK: atom_id → atom.atom_id

TABLE: molecule
  molecule_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  label (TEXT)  e.g. '+', '-'

Follow the Hint above, explore the DB if needed, test your SQL, then FINAL("your sql").

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.sample_values("atom", "atom_id")
```
```python
db.sample_values("atom", "atom_id")
```
```python
db.sample_values("molecule", "label")
```
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT a.element, COUNT(*) AS tally
FROM atom a
JOIN molecule m ON a.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
WHERE m.label = '+'
  AND substr(a.atom_id, 7, 1) = '4'
GROUP BY a.element
""")
```
FINAL("SELECT a.element, COUNT(*) AS tally FROM atom a JOIN molecule m ON a.molecule_id = m.molecule_id WHERE m.label = '+' AND substr(a.atom_id, 7, 1) = '4' GROUP BY a.element")

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence

  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.

task prompt (8385 chars)
QUESTION: Which are the cards that have incredibly powerful foils.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  incredibly poweful foils refers to cardKingdomFoilId is not null AND cardKingdomId is not null


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: card_games

TABLE: cards
  id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  artist (TEXT)  e.g. 'Pete Venters', 'Volkan Baǵa'
  asciiName (TEXT)  e.g. 'El-Hajjaj', 'Junun Efreet'
  availability (TEXT)  e.g. 'mtgo,paper', 'paper'

  colorIdentity (TEXT)  e.g. 'W', 'B'
  colorIndicator (TEXT)  e.g. 'U', 'G'
  colors (TEXT)  e.g. 'W', 'B'
  convertedManaCost (REAL)  e.g. 7.0, 5.0
  duelDeck (TEXT)  e.g. 'a', 'b'

  faceConvertedManaCost (REAL)  e.g. 4.0, 5.0
  faceName (TEXT)  e.g. 'Dusk', 'Dawn'

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence

  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.

task prompt (8445 chars)
QUESTION: List all the mythic rarity print cards banned in gladiator format.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  mythic rarity printing refers to rarity = 'mythic'; card banned refers to status = 'Banned'; in gladiator format refers to format = 'gladiator';


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: card_games

TABLE: cards
  id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  artist (TEXT)  e.g. 'Pete Venters', 'Volkan Baǵa'
  asciiName (TEXT)  e.g. 'El-Hajjaj', 'Junun Efreet'
  availability (TEXT)  e.g. 'mtgo,paper', 'paper'

  faceName (TEXT)  e.g. 'Dusk', 'Dawn'

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT c.name, c.setCode, c.rarity, l.format, l.status
FROM cards c
JOIN legalities l ON c.uuid = l.uuid
WHERE c.rarity = 'mythic'
  AND l.status = 'Banned'
  AND l.format = 'gladiator'
ORDER BY c.name, c.setCode;
""")
```

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT c.name, c.setCode, c.rarity, l.format, l.status
FROM cards c
JOIN legalities l ON c.uuid = l.uuid
WHERE c.rarity = 'mythic'
  AND l.status = 'Banned'
  AND l.format = 'gladiator'
ORDER BY c.name, c.setCode;
""")
```
FINAL("SELECT DISTINCT c.name
FROM cards AS c
JOIN legalities AS l ON c.uuid = l.uuid
WHERE c.rarity = 'mythic'
  AND l.status = 'Banned'
  AND l.format = 'gladiator'
ORDER BY c.name;")
