task prompt (4765 chars)
QUESTION: What's Angela Sanders's major?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  Angela Sanders is the full name; full name refers to first_name, last_name; major refers to major_name.


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: student_club

TABLE: attendance
  link_to_event (TEXT) [PK]
  link_to_member (TEXT) [PK]
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id
  FK: link_to_event → event.event_id

TABLE: budget
  budget_id (TEXT) [PK]
  category (TEXT)  e.g. 'Advertisement', 'Food'
  spent (REAL)  e.g. 67.81, 121.14
  remaining (REAL)  e.g. 7.19, 28.86
  amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 75, 150
  event_status (TEXT)  e.g. 'Closed', 'Open'
  link_to_event (TEXT)  e.g. 'recI43CzsZ0Q625ma', 'recggMW2eyCYceNcy'
  FK: link_to_event → event.event_id

TABLE: event
  event_id (TEXT) [PK]
  event_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'March Meeting', 'Officers meeting - January'
  event_date (TEXT)  e.g. '2020-03-10T12:00:00', '2020-01-14T09:30:00'
  type (TEXT)  e.g. 'Meeting', 'Election'
  notes (TEXT)  e.g. 'All active members can vote...', "Attend Women's soccer game ..."
  location (TEXT)  e.g. 'MU 215', 'Campus Soccer/Lacrosse stadium'
  status (TEXT)  e.g. 'Open', 'Planning'

TABLE: expense
  expense_id (TEXT) [PK]
  expense_description (TEXT)  e.g. 'Post Cards, Posters', 'Water, Cookies'
  expense_date (TEXT)  e.g. '2019-08-20', '2019-10-08'
  cost (REAL)  e.g. 122.06, 20.2
  approved (TEXT)  e.g. 'true'
  link_to_member (TEXT)  e.g. 'rec4BLdZHS2Blfp4v', 'recro8T1MPMwRadVH'
  link_to_budget (TEXT)  e.g. 'recvKTAWAFKkVNnXQ', 'recy8KY5bUdzF81vv'
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id
  FK: link_to_budget → budget.budget_id

TABLE: income
  income_id (TEXT) [PK]
  date_received (TEXT)  e.g. '2019-10-17', '2019-09-04'
  amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 50, 200
  source (TEXT)  e.g. 'Dues', 'Fundraising'
  notes (TEXT)  e.g. 'Secured donations to help p...', 'Annual funding from Student...'
  link_to_member (TEXT)  e.g. 'reccW7q1KkhSKZsea', 'recTjHY5xXhvkCdVT'
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id

TABLE: major
  major_id (TEXT) [PK]
  major_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Outdoor Product Design and ...', 'Agricultural Communication'
  department (TEXT)  e.g. 'School of Applied Sciences,...', 'Watershed Sciences Department'
  college (TEXT)  e.g. 'College of Agriculture and ...', 'College of Natural Resources'

TABLE: member
  member_id (TEXT) [PK]
  first_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Angela', 'Grant'
  last_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Sanders', 'Gilmour'
  email (TEXT)  e.g. 'angela.sanders@lpu.edu', 'grant.gilmour@lpu.edu'
  position (TEXT)  e.g. 'Member', 'Inactive'
  t_shirt_size (TEXT)  e.g. 'Medium', 'X-Large'
  phone (TEXT)  e.g. '(651) 928-4507', '403-555-1310'
  zip (INTEGER)  e.g. 55108, 29440
  link_to_major (TEXT)  e.g. 'recxK3MHQFbR9J5uO', 'rec7BxKpjJ7bNph3O'
  FK: zip → zip_code.zip_code
  FK: link_to_major → major.major_id

TABLE: zip_code
  zip_code (INTEGER) [PK]
  type (TEXT)  e.g. 'Unique', 'Standard'
  city (TEXT)  e.g. 'Holtsville', 'Adjuntas'
  county (TEXT)  e.g. 'Suffolk County', 'Adjuntas Municipio'
  state (TEXT)  e.g. 'New York', 'Puerto Rico'
  short_state (TEXT)  e.g. 'NY', 'PR'

Follow the Hint above, explore the DB if needed, test your SQL, then FINAL("your sql").

- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.

task prompt (4948 chars)
QUESTION: Calculate the difference of the total amount spent in all events by the Student_Club in year 2019 and 2020.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  The first 4 strings of the event_date values in the event table can represent year; The difference of the total amount spent = SUBTRACT(spent where YEAR(event_date) = 2019, spent where YEAR(event_date) = 2020)


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.

🤖 model turn:

I’ll test the exact SQL against the database first.
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT
  COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN SUBSTR(e.event_date, 1, 4) = '2019' THEN b.spent END), 0)
  - COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN SUBSTR(e.event_date, 1, 4) = '2020' THEN b.spent END), 0) AS difference
FROM budget AS b
JOIN event AS e
  ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id;
""")
```
SELECT COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN SUBSTR(e.event_date, 1, 4) = '2019' THEN b.spent ELSE 0 END), 0)
     - COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN SUBSTR(e.event_date, 1, 4) = '2020' THEN b.spent ELSE 0 END), 0) AS difference
FROM budget AS b
JOIN event AS e
  ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id;

- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: student_club

TABLE: attendance
  link_to_event (TEXT) [PK]
  link_to_member (TEXT) [PK]
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id
  FK: link_to_event → event.event_id

TABLE: budget
  budget_id (TEXT) [PK]
  category (TEXT)  e.g. 'Advertisement', 'Food'
  spent (REAL)  e.g. 67.81, 121.14
  remaining (REAL)  e.g. 7.19, 28.86
  amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 75, 150
  event_status (TEXT)  e.g. 'Closed', 'Open'
  link_to_event (TEXT)  e.g. 'recI43CzsZ0Q625ma', 'recggMW2eyCYceNcy'
  FK: link_to_event → event.event_id

TABLE: event
  event_id (TEXT) [PK]
  event_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'March Meeting', 'Officers meeting - January'
  event_date (TEXT)  e.g. '2020-03-10T12:00:00', '2020-01-14T09:30:00'
  type (TEXT)  e.g. 'Meeting', 'Election'
  notes (TEXT)  e.g. 'All active members can vote...', "Attend Women's soccer game ..."
  location (TEXT)  e.g. 'MU 215', 'Campus Soccer/Lacrosse stadium'
  status (TEXT)  e.g. 'Open', 'Planning'

TABLE: expense
  expense_id (TEXT) [PK]
  expense_description (TEXT)  e.g. 'Post Cards, Posters', 'Water, Cookies'
  expense_date (TEXT)  e.g. '2019-08-20', '2019-10-08'
  cost (REAL)  e.g. 122.06, 20.2
  approved (TEXT)  e.g. 'true'
  link_to_member (TEXT)  e.g. 'rec4BLdZHS2Blfp4v', 'recro8T1MPMwRadVH'
  link_to_budget (TEXT)  e.g. 'recvKTAWAFKkVNnXQ', 'recy8KY5bUdzF81vv'
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id
  FK: link_to_budget → budget.budget_id

TABLE: income
  income_id (TEXT) [PK]
  date_received (TEXT)  e.g. '2019-10-17', '2019-09-04'
  amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 50, 200
  source (TEXT)  e.g. 'Dues', 'Fundraising'
  notes (TEXT)  e.g. 'Secured donations to help p...', 'Annual funding from Student...'
  link_to_member (TEXT)  e.g. 'reccW7q1KkhSKZsea', 'recTjHY5xXhvkCdVT'
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id

TABLE: major
  major_id (TEXT) [PK]
  major_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Outdoor Product Design and ...', 'Agricultural Communication'
  department (TEXT)  e.g. 'School of Applied Sciences,...', 'Watershed Sciences Department'
  college (TEXT)  e.g. 'College of Agriculture and ...', 'College of Natural Resources'

TABLE: member
  member_id (TEXT) [PK]
  first_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Angela', 'Grant'
  last_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Sanders', 'Gilmour'
  email (TEXT)  e.g. 'angela.sanders@lpu.edu', 'grant.gilmour@lpu.edu'
  position (TEXT)  e.g. 'Member', 'Inactive'
  t_shirt_size (TEXT)  e.g. 'Medium', 'X-Large'
  phone (TEXT)  e.g. '(651) 928-4507', '403-555-1310'
  zip (INTEGER)  e.g. 55108, 29440
  link_to_major (TEXT)  e.g. 'recxK3MHQFbR9J5uO', 'rec7BxKpjJ7bNph3O'
  FK: zip → zip_code.zip_code
  FK: link_to_major → major.major_id

TABLE: zip_code
  zip_code (INTEGER) [PK]
  type (TEXT)  e.g. 'Unique', 'Standard'
  city (TEXT)  e.g. 'Holtsville', 'Adjuntas'
  county (TEXT)  e.g. 'Suffolk County', 'Adjuntas Municipio'
  state (TEXT)  e.g. 'New York', 'Puerto Rico'
  short_state (TEXT)  e.g. 'NY', 'PR'

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT b.event_status
FROM expense e
JOIN budget b ON e.link_to_budget = b.budget_id
WHERE e.expense_description = 'Post Cards, Posters'
  AND e.expense_date = '2019-8-20'
""")
```

```python

SELECT b.event_status
FROM expense e
JOIN budget b ON e.link_to_budget = b.budget_id
WHERE e.expense_description = 'Post Cards, Posters'
  AND e.expense_date = '2019-8-20'
""")
```
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT DISTINCT b.event_status
FROM expense AS e
JOIN budget AS b ON e.link_to_budget = b.budget_id
WHERE e.expense_description = 'Post Cards, Posters'
  AND e.expense_date = '2019-8-20'
""")
```

FROM expense AS e
JOIN budget AS b ON e.link_to_budget = b.budget_id
WHERE e.expense_description = 'Post Cards, Posters'
  AND e.expense_date = '2019-8-20';

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence
     is needed.
  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.
  3. Submit plain text FINAL("YOUR SQL HERE") without a code block.

task prompt (4823 chars)
QUESTION: Which student has been entrusted to manage the budget for the Yearly Kickoff?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  name of students means the full name; full name refers to first_name, last_name;'Yearly Kickoff' is an event name;


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: student_club

TABLE: attendance
  link_to_event (TEXT) [PK]
  link_to_member (TEXT) [PK]
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id
  FK: link_to_event → event.event_id

TABLE: budget
  budget_id (TEXT) [PK]
  category (TEXT)  e.g. 'Advertisement', 'Food'
  spent (REAL)  e.g. 67.81, 121.14
  remaining (REAL)  e.g. 7.19, 28.86
  amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 75, 150
  event_status (TEXT)  e.g. 'Closed', 'Open'
  link_to_event (TEXT)  e.g. 'recI43CzsZ0Q625ma', 'recggMW2eyCYceNcy'
  FK: link_to_event → event.event_id

TABLE: event
  event_id (TEXT) [PK]
  event_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'March Meeting', 'Officers meeting - January'
  event_date (TEXT)  e.g. '2020-03-10T12:00:00', '2020-01-14T09:30:00'
  type (TEXT)  e.g. 'Meeting', 'Election'
  notes (TEXT)  e.g. 'All active members can vote...', "Attend Women's soccer game ..."
  location (TEXT)  e.g. 'MU 215', 'Campus Soccer/Lacrosse stadium'
  status (TEXT)  e.g. 'Open', 'Planning'

TABLE: expense
  expense_id (TEXT) [PK]
  expense_description (TEXT)  e.g. 'Post Cards, Posters', 'Water, Cookies'
  expense_date (TEXT)  e.g. '2019-08-20', '2019-10-08'
  cost (REAL)  e.g. 122.06, 20.2
  approved (TEXT)  e.g. 'true'
  link_to_member (TEXT)  e.g. 'rec4BLdZHS2Blfp4v', 'recro8T1MPMwRadVH'
  link_to_budget (TEXT)  e.g. 'recvKTAWAFKkVNnXQ', 'recy8KY5bUdzF81vv'
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id
  FK: link_to_budget → budget.budget_id

TABLE: income
  income_id (TEXT) [PK]
  date_received (TEXT)  e.g. '2019-10-17', '2019-09-04'
  amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 50, 200
  source (TEXT)  e.g. 'Dues', 'Fundraising'
  notes (TEXT)  e.g. 'Secured donations to help p...', 'Annual funding from Student...'
  link_to_member (TEXT)  e.g. 'reccW7q1KkhSKZsea', 'recTjHY5xXhvkCdVT'
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id

TABLE: major
  major_id (TEXT) [PK]
  major_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Outdoor Product Design and ...', 'Agricultural Communication'
  department (TEXT)  e.g. 'School of Applied Sciences,...', 'Watershed Sciences Department'
  college (TEXT)  e.g. 'College of Agriculture and ...', 'College of Natural Resources'

  FK: zip → zip_code.zip_code
  FK: link_to_major → major.major_id

task prompt (4872 chars)
QUESTION: Write the full names of students who received funds on the date of 9/9/2019 and include the amount received.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  full name refers to first_name, last_name, amount of funds received refers to amount, received funds on date refers to date_received


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: student_club

TABLE: attendance
  link_to_event (TEXT) [PK]
  link_to_member (TEXT) [PK]
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id
  FK: link_to_event → event.event_id

TABLE: budget
  budget_id (TEXT) [PK]
  category (TEXT)  e.g. 'Advertisement', 'Food'
  spent (REAL)  e.g. 67.81, 121.14
  remaining (REAL)  e.g. 7.19, 28.86
  amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 75, 150
  event_status (TEXT)  e.g. 'Closed', 'Open'
  link_to_event (TEXT)  e.g. 'recI43CzsZ0Q625ma', 'recggMW2eyCYceNcy'
  FK: link_to_event → event.event_id

TABLE: event
  event_id (TEXT) [PK]
  event_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'March Meeting', 'Officers meeting - January'
  event_date (TEXT)  e.g. '2020-03-10T12:00:00', '2020-01-14T09:30:00'
  type (TEXT)  e.g. 'Meeting', 'Election'
  notes (TEXT)  e.g. 'All active members can vote...', "Attend Women's soccer game ..."
  location (TEXT)  e.g. 'MU 215', 'Campus Soccer/Lacrosse stadium'
  status (TEXT)  e.g. 'Open', 'Planning'

TABLE: expense
  expense_id (TEXT) [PK]
  expense_description (TEXT)  e.g. 'Post Cards, Posters', 'Water, Cookies'
  expense_date (TEXT)  e.g. '2019-08-20', '2019-10-08'
  cost (REAL)  e.g. 122.06, 20.2
  approved (TEXT)  e.g. 'true'
  link_to_member (TEXT)  e.g. 'rec4BLdZHS2Blfp4v', 'recro8T1MPMwRadVH'
  link_to_budget (TEXT)  e.g. 'recvKTAWAFKkVNnXQ', 'recy8KY5bUdzF81vv'
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id
  FK: link_to_budget → budget.budget_id

TABLE: income
  income_id (TEXT) [PK]
  date_received (TEXT)  e.g. '2019-10-17', '2019-09-04'
  amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 50, 200
  source (TEXT)  e.g. 'Dues', 'Fundraising'
  notes (TEXT)  e.g. 'Secured donations to help p...', 'Annual funding from Student...'
  link_to_member (TEXT)  e.g. 'reccW7q1KkhSKZsea', 'recTjHY5xXhvkCdVT'
  FK: link_to_member → member.member_id

TABLE: major
  major_id (TEXT) [PK]
  major_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Outdoor Product Design and ...', 'Agricultural Communication'
  department (TEXT)  e.g. 'School of Applied Sciences,...', 'Watershed Sciences Department'
  college (TEXT)  e.g. 'College of Agriculture and ...', 'College of Natural Resources'

TABLE: member
  member_id (TEXT) [PK]
  first_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Angela', 'Grant'
  last_name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Sanders', 'Gilmour'
  email (TEXT)  e.g. 'angela.sanders@lpu.edu', 'grant.gilmour@lpu.edu'
  position (TEXT)  e.g. 'Member', 'Inactive'
  t_shirt_size (TEXT)  e.g. 'Medium', 'X-Large'
  phone (TEXT)  e.g. '(651) 928-4507', '403-555-1310'
  zip (INTEGER)  e.g. 55108, 29440
  link_to_major (TEXT)  e.g. 'recxK3MHQFbR9J5uO', 'rec7BxKpjJ7bNph3O'
  FK: zip → zip_code.zip_code
  FK: link_to_major → major.major_id

TABLE: zip_code
  zip_code (INTEGER) [PK]
  type (TEXT)  e.g. 'Unique', 'Standard'
  city (TEXT)  e.g. 'Holtsville', 'Adjuntas'
  county (TEXT)  e.g. 'Suffolk County', 'Adjuntas Municipio'
  state (TEXT)  e.g. 'New York', 'Puerto Rico'
  short_state (TEXT)  e.g. 'NY', 'PR'

task prompt (3069 chars)
QUESTION: What is the ratio of customers who pay in EUR against customers who pay in CZK?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  ratio of customers who pay in EUR against customers who pay in CZK = count(Currency = 'EUR') / count(Currency = 'CZK').


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: debit_card_specializing

TABLE: customers
  CustomerID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Segment (TEXT)  e.g. 'SME', 'LAM'
  Currency (TEXT)  e.g. 'EUR', 'CZK'

TABLE: gasstations
  GasStationID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  ChainID (INTEGER)  e.g. 13, 6
  Country (TEXT)  e.g. 'CZE', 'SVK'
  Segment (TEXT)  e.g. 'Value for money', 'Premium'

TABLE: products
  ProductID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Description (TEXT)  e.g. 'Rucní zadání', 'Nafta'

TABLE: sqlite_sequence
  name (UNKNOWN)  e.g. 'transactions_1k'
  seq (UNKNOWN)  e.g. 1000

TABLE: transactions_1k
  TransactionID (INTEGER) [PK]
  Date (DATE)  e.g. '2012-08-24', '2012-08-23'
  Time (TEXT)  e.g. '09:41:00', '10:03:00'
  CustomerID (INTEGER)  e.g. 31543, 46707
  CardID (INTEGER)  e.g. 486621, 550134
  GasStationID (INTEGER)  e.g. 3704, 656
  ProductID (INTEGER)  e.g. 2, 23
  Amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 28, 18
  Price (REAL)  e.g. 672.64, 430.72

TABLE: yearmonth
  CustomerID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Date (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Consumption (REAL)  e.g. 528.3, 1598.28
  FK: CustomerID → customers.None
  FK: CustomerID → customers.None

```python
db.execute("SELECT COUNT(CASE WHEN Currency = 'EUR' THEN 1 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(CASE WHEN Currency = 'CZK' THEN 1 END) AS ratio FROM customers;")
```
```python
db.execute("SELECT COUNT(CASE WHEN Currency = 'EUR' THEN 1 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(CASE WHEN Currency = 'CZK' THEN 1 END) AS ratio FROM customers;")
```
```python
db.execute("SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN Currency = 'EUR' THEN 1 END) AS REAL) / NULLIF(COUNT(CASE WHEN Currency = 'CZK' THEN 1 END), 0) AS ratio FROM customers;")
```
FINAL("SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN Currency = 'EUR' THEN 1 END) AS REAL) / NULLIF(COUNT(CASE WHEN Currency = 'CZK' THEN 1 END), 0) AS ratio FROM customers;")

- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.

DATABASE: debit_card_specializing

TABLE: customers
  CustomerID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Segment (TEXT)  e.g. 'SME', 'LAM'
  Currency (TEXT)  e.g. 'EUR', 'CZK'

TABLE: gasstations

  ChainID (INTEGER)  e.g. 13, 6

```python
db.sample_values("yearmonth", "Date")
```
```python
db.sample_values("yearmonth", "Date")
```
FINAL("WITH yearly AS (SELECT y.CustomerID, SUM(y.Consumption) AS total_consumption FROM yearmonth y JOIN customers c ON c.CustomerID = y.CustomerID WHERE c.Segment = 'LAM' AND substr(y.Date, 1, 4) = '2012' GROUP BY y.CustomerID) SELECT CustomerID FROM yearly ORDER BY total_consumption ASC, CustomerID ASC LIMIT 1;")

⚙️ observation:

{'table': 'yearmonth', 'column': 'Date', 'values': ['201112', '201201', '201202', '201203', '201204', '201205', '201206', '201207', '201208', '201209'], 'error': None}

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.execute("""
WITH yearly AS (
  SELECT y.CustomerID, SUM(y.Consumption) AS total_consumption
  FROM yearmonth y
  JOIN customers c ON c.CustomerID = y.CustomerID
  WHERE c.Segment = 'LAM'
    AND y.Date BETWEEN '201201' AND '201212'
  GROUP BY y.CustomerID
)
SELECT CustomerID
FROM yearly
ORDER BY total_consumption ASC, CustomerID ASC
LIMIT 1;
""")
```
FINAL("WITH yearly AS (SELECT y.CustomerID, SUM(y.Consumption) AS total_consumption FROM yearmonth y JOIN customers c ON c.CustomerID = y.CustomerID WHERE c.Segment = 'LAM' AND y.Date BETWEEN '201201' AND '201212' GROUP BY y.CustomerID) SELECT CustomerID FROM yearly ORDER BY total_consumption ASC, CustomerID ASC LIMIT 1;")

    ],
    "rows": [

        47273
      ]
    ],

}

You are a Text-to-SQL agent. Produce one read-only SQLite SELECT query that answers
the user question using only the provided question, evidence, schema, train examples,

AVAILABLE TOOLS (inside ```python blocks):

  db.sample_values("table", "column")

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence
     is needed.
  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.
  3. Submit plain text FINAL("YOUR SQL HERE") without a code block.
  4. Do not place tool code and FINAL in the same response.
  5. Do not use capabilities that are not explicitly listed.
task prompt (3130 chars)
QUESTION: What was the gas consumption peak month for SME customers in 2013?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  Year 2013 can be presented as Between 201301 And 201312; The first 4 strings of the Date values in the yearmonth table can represent year; The 5th and 6th string of the date can refer to month.


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: debit_card_specializing

TABLE: customers
  CustomerID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Segment (TEXT)  e.g. 'SME', 'LAM'
  Currency (TEXT)  e.g. 'EUR', 'CZK'

TABLE: gasstations
  GasStationID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  ChainID (INTEGER)  e.g. 13, 6
  Country (TEXT)  e.g. 'CZE', 'SVK'
  Segment (TEXT)  e.g. 'Value for money', 'Premium'

TABLE: products
  ProductID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Description (TEXT)  e.g. 'Rucní zadání', 'Nafta'

TABLE: sqlite_sequence
  name (UNKNOWN)  e.g. 'transactions_1k'
  seq (UNKNOWN)  e.g. 1000

TABLE: transactions_1k
  TransactionID (INTEGER) [PK]
  Date (DATE)  e.g. '2012-08-24', '2012-08-23'
  Time (TEXT)  e.g. '09:41:00', '10:03:00'
  CustomerID (INTEGER)  e.g. 31543, 46707
  CardID (INTEGER)  e.g. 486621, 550134
  GasStationID (INTEGER)  e.g. 3704, 656
  ProductID (INTEGER)  e.g. 2, 23
  Amount (INTEGER)  e.g. 28, 18
  Price (REAL)  e.g. 672.64, 430.72

TABLE: yearmonth
  CustomerID (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Date (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Consumption (REAL)  e.g. 528.3, 1598.28
  FK: CustomerID → customers.None
  FK: CustomerID → customers.None

- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.

- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.

task prompt (2515 chars)

HINT (use these definitions exactly):

TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.

- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.

Schema:
DATABASE: toxicology

TABLE: atom
  atom_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  molecule_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000', 'TR001'
  element (TEXT)  e.g. 'cl', 'c'
  FK: molecule_id → molecule.molecule_id

TABLE: bond
  bond_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  molecule_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000', 'TR001'
  bond_type (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '='
  FK: molecule_id → molecule.molecule_id

TABLE: connected
  atom_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  atom_id2 (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  bond_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000_1_2', 'TR000_2_3'
  FK: bond_id → bond.bond_id
  FK: atom_id2 → atom.atom_id
  FK: atom_id → atom.atom_id

TABLE: molecule
  molecule_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  label (TEXT)  e.g. '+', '-'

bird_282 [toxicology / challenging] ✔ correct — What is the ratio of Hydrogen elements in molecule ID TR006? List the ratio with its label.
system prompt (679 chars)
You are a Text-to-SQL agent. Produce one read-only SQLite SELECT query that answers
the user question using only the provided question, evidence, schema, train examples,
and observable database results.

AVAILABLE TOOLS (inside ```python blocks):
  db.execute("SQL")
  db.sample_values("table", "column")

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence
     is needed.
  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.
  3. Submit plain text FINAL("YOUR SQL HERE") without a code block.
  4. Do not place tool code and FINAL in the same response.
  5. Do not use capabilities that are not explicitly listed.
task prompt (2715 chars)
QUESTION: What is the ratio of Hydrogen elements in molecule ID TR006? List the ratio with its label.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  hydrogen refers to element = 'h'; ratio = DIVIDE(SUM(element = 'h'), count(element)) where molecule_id = 'TR006' ; label = '+' mean molecules are carcinogenic; label = '-' means molecules are non-carcinogenic


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: toxicology

TABLE: atom
  atom_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  molecule_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000', 'TR001'
  element (TEXT)  e.g. 'cl', 'c'
  FK: molecule_id → molecule.molecule_id

TABLE: bond
  bond_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  molecule_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000', 'TR001'
  bond_type (TEXT)  e.g. '-', '='
  FK: molecule_id → molecule.molecule_id

TABLE: connected
  atom_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  atom_id2 (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  bond_id (TEXT)  e.g. 'TR000_1_2', 'TR000_2_3'
  FK: bond_id → bond.bond_id
  FK: atom_id2 → atom.atom_id
  FK: atom_id → atom.atom_id

TABLE: molecule
  molecule_id (TEXT) [PK, NOT NULL]
  label (TEXT)  e.g. '+', '-'

Follow the Hint above, explore the DB if needed, test your SQL, then FINAL("your sql").

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.execute("SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN element = 'h' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / NULLIF(COUNT(element), 0) AS ratio, m.label FROM atom a JOIN molecule m ON a.molecule_id = m.molecule_id WHERE a.molecule_id = 'TR006';")
```
FINAL("SELECT SUM(a.element = 'h') * 1.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(a.element), 0) AS ratio, m.label FROM atom a JOIN molecule m ON a.molecule_id = m.molecule_id WHERE a.molecule_id = 'TR006' GROUP BY m.label;")

GOLD SQL: WITH SubQuery AS (SELECT DISTINCT T1.atom_id, T1.element, T1.molecule_id, T2.label FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T2.molecule_id = 'TR006') SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN element = 'h' THEN atom_id ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) / (CASE WHEN COUNT(atom_id) = 0 THEN NULL ELSE COUNT(atom_id) END) AS ratio, label FROM SubQuery GROUP BY label

DATABASE: card_games

TABLE: cards
  id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  artist (TEXT)  e.g. 'Pete Venters', 'Volkan Baǵa'
  asciiName (TEXT)  e.g. 'El-Hajjaj', 'Junun Efreet'

  convertedManaCost (REAL)  e.g. 7.0, 5.0
  duelDeck (TEXT)  e.g. 'a', 'b'
  edhrecRank (INTEGER)  e.g. 15650, 12702
  faceConvertedManaCost (REAL)  e.g. 4.0, 5.0
  faceName (TEXT)  e.g. 'Dusk', 'Dawn'

task prompt (8380 chars)
QUESTION: How many cards with original type of "Summon - Angel" have subtype other than "Angel"?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  subtype other than Angel refers to subtypes is not 'Angel';


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.

task prompt (6908 chars)
QUESTION: What is the average number of test takers from Fresno schools that opened between 1/1/1980 and 12/31/1980?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  between 1/1/1980 and 12/31/1980 means the year = 1980


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt, AVG(s.NumTstTakr) AS avg_takers
FROM schools AS sch
JOIN satscores AS s
  ON sch.CDSCode = s.cds
WHERE sch.County = 'Fresno'
  AND strftime('%Y', sch.OpenDate) = '1980';
""")
```
FINAL("SELECT AVG(s.NumTstTakr) AS average_num_test_takers
FROM schools AS sch
JOIN satscores AS s
  ON sch.CDSCode = s.cds
WHERE sch.County = 'Fresno'
  AND strftime('%Y', sch.OpenDate) = '1980';")

- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.

TABLE: foreign_data
  id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  flavorText (

🤖 model turn:

I’ll verify the exact filter and then return the SQL.
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT DISTINCT c.name, f.language
FROM cards c
JOIN foreign_data f ON f.uuid = c.uuid
WHERE c.originalType = 'Artifact'
  AND c.colors = 'B'
ORDER BY c.name, f.language
LIMIT 20;
""")
```
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT DISTINCT c.name, f.language
FROM cards c
JOIN foreign_data f ON f.uuid = c.uuid
WHERE c.originalType = 'Artifact'
  AND c.colors = 'B'
ORDER BY c.name, f.language
LIMIT 20;
""")
```
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT DISTINCT c.name, f.language
FROM cards c
JOIN foreign_data f ON f.uuid = c.uuid
WHERE c.originalType = 'Artifact'
  AND c.colors = 'B'
ORDER BY c.name, f.language
LIMIT 20;
""")
```
FINAL("SELECT DISTINCT c.name FROM cards AS c WHERE c.originalType = 'Artifact' AND c.colors = 'B' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM foreign_data AS f WHERE f.uuid = c.uuid) ORDER BY c.name;")

task prompt (8477 chars)
QUESTION: What is the mana cost of cards with a normal layout, a 2003 frame version, with a black border color, and available in paper and mtgo?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  available in paper and mtgo refers to availability = 'mtgo,paper'; frameVersion = 2003;borderColor = 'black'


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: card_games

TABLE: cards
  id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  artist (TEXT)  e.g. 'Pete Venters', 'Volkan Baǵa'

- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.

🤖 model turn:

I’ll check the converted mana costs for both named cards, then return the card with the higher value.
```python
db.execute("SELECT name, convertedManaCost FROM cards WHERE name IN ('Serra Angel', 'Shrine Keeper') ORDER BY name;")
```

```python
db.execute("SELECT name, convertedManaCost FROM cards WHERE name IN ('Serra Angel', 'Shrine Keeper') ORDER BY name;")
```
```python
db.execute("SELECT name, convertedManaCost FROM cards WHERE name IN ('Serra Angel', 'Shrine Keeper') ORDER BY name;")
```
FINAL("SELECT name FROM (SELECT name, MAX(convertedManaCost) AS cmc FROM cards WHERE name IN ('Serra Angel', 'Shrine Keeper') GROUP BY name) ORDER BY cmc DESC, name LIMIT 1;")

system prompt (679 chars)

and observable database results.

AVAILABLE TOOLS (inside ```python blocks):
  db.execute("SQL")
  db.sample_values("table", "column")

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence
     is needed.
  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.
  3. Submit plain text FINAL("YOUR SQL HERE") without a code block.

task prompt (6911 chars)
QUESTION: Which state special schools have the highest number of enrollees from grades 1 through 12?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  State Special Schools refers to DOC = 31; Grades 1 through 12 means K-12


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.

🤖 model turn:

I’ll check the DOC=31 schools and their K-12 enrollment, then confirm the max before writing the final query.
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT s.School, s.DOC, f.`Enrollment (K-12)` AS enrollment
FROM schools s
JOIN frpm f ON f.CDSCode = s.CDSCode
WHERE s.DOC = '31'
ORDER BY enrollment DESC
LIMIT 10;
""")
```
to=python
db.execute("""
SELECT s.School, s.DOC, f.`Enrollment (K-12)` AS enrollment
FROM schools s
JOIN frpm f ON f.CDSCode = s.CDSCode
WHERE s.DOC = '31'
ORDER BY enrollment DESC
LIMIT 10;
""")
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT s.School, s.DOC, f.`Enrollment (K-12)` AS enrollment
FROM schools s
JOIN frpm f ON f.CDSCode = s.CDSCode
WHERE s.DOC = '31'
ORDER BY enrollment DESC
LIMIT 10;
""")
```
```python
db.execute("SELECT 1;")
```
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT s.School, f.`Enrollment (K-12)` AS enrollment
FROM schools s
JOIN frpm f ON f.CDSCode = s.CDSCode
WHERE s.DOC = '31'
  AND f.`Enrollment (K-12)` = (
    SELECT MAX(f2.`Enrollment (K-12)`)
    FROM schools s2
    JOIN frpm f2 ON f2.CDSCode = s2.CDSCode
    WHERE s2.DOC = '31'
  )
ORDER BY s.School;
""")
```
FINAL("SELECT s.School FROM schools s JOIN frpm f ON f.CDSCode = s.CDSCode WHERE s.DOC = '31' AND f.`Enrollment (K-12)` = (SELECT MAX(f2.`Enrollment (K-12)`) FROM schools s2 JOIN frpm f2 ON f2.CDSCode = s2.CDSCode WHERE s2.DOC = '31') ORDER BY s.School;")

Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.

Schema:
DATABASE: card_games

TABLE: cards
  id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  artist (TEXT)  e.g. 'Pete Venters', 'Volkan Baǵa'
  asciiName (TEXT)  e.g. 'El-Hajjaj', 'Junun Efreet'
  availability (TEXT)  e.g. 'mtgo,paper', 'paper'

  convertedManaCost (REAL)  e.g. 7.0, 5.0
  duelDeck (TEXT)  e.g. 'a', 'b'
  edhrecRank (INTEGER)  e.g. 15650, 12702
  faceConvertedManaCost (REAL)  e.g. 4.0, 5.0
  faceName (TEXT)  e.g. 'Dusk', 'Dawn'
  flavorName (TEXT)  e.g. 'Godzilla, King of the Monsters', 'King Caesar, Ancient Guardian'
  flavorText (TEXT)  e.g. 'Every tear shed is a drop o...', 'The perfect antidote for a ...'
  frameEffects (TEXT)  e.g. 'legendary', 'nyxtouched'
  frameVersion (TEXT)  e.g. '2003', '1993'

  isOversized (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 0, 1

  name (TEXT)  e.g. "Ancestor's Chosen", 'Angel of Mercy'
  number (TEXT)  e.g. '1', '2'

TABLE: foreign_data
  id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  flavorText (TEXT)  e.g. '„Es i

🤖 model turn:

I’ll check which set code(s) contain “Ancestor’s Chosen” and then look up the Italian set translation.
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT c.setCode, s.name, st.translation
FROM cards c
JOIN sets s ON s.code = c.setCode
JOIN set_translations st ON st.setCode = s.code
WHERE c.name = 'Ancestor''s Chosen'
  AND st.language = 'Italian';
""")
```
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT c.setCode, s.name, st.translation
FROM cards c
JOIN sets s ON s.code = c.setCode
JOIN set_translations st ON st.setCode = s.code
WHERE c.name = 'Ancestor''s Chosen'
  AND st.language = 'Italian';
""")
```
FINAL("SELECT DISTINCT st.translation FROM cards c JOIN set_translations st ON st.setCode = c.setCode WHERE c.name = 'Ancestor''s Chosen' AND st.language = 'Italian';")

task prompt (5990 chars)
QUESTION: Which user has a higher reputation, Harlan or Jarrod Dixon?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  "Harlan" and "Jarrod Dixon" are both DisplayName; highest reputation refers to Max(Reputation)


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: codebase_community

TABLE: badges
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  UserId (INTEGER)  e.g. 5, 6
  Name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Teacher', 'Student'
  Date (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 19:39:07.0', '2010-07-19 19:39:08.0'
  FK: UserId → users.Id

TABLE: comments
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  PostId (INTEGER)  e.g. 3, 5
  Score (INTEGER)  e.g. 5, 0
  Text (TEXT)  e.g. 'Could be a poster child fo ...', 'Yes, R is nice- but WHY is ...'
  CreationDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 19:15:52.0', '2010-07-19 19:16:14.0'
  UserId (INTEGER)  e.g. 13, 37
  UserDisplayName (TEXT)  e.g. 'user28', 'Statprof'
  FK: UserId → users.Id
  FK: PostId → posts.Id

TABLE: postHistory
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  PostHistoryTypeId (INTEGER)  e.g. 2, 1

  FK: PostId → posts.Id

TABLE: users

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT DisplayName, MAX(Reputation) AS MaxReputation
FROM users
WHERE DisplayName IN ('Harlan', 'Jarrod Dixon')
GROUP BY DisplayName;
""")
```
```python
# execute
db.execute("SELECT DisplayName, MAX(Reputation) AS MaxReputation FROM users WHERE DisplayName IN ('Harlan', 'Jarrod Dixon') GROUP BY DisplayName;")
```
```python
db.execute("SELECT DisplayName, MAX(Reputation) AS MaxReputation FROM users WHERE DisplayName IN ('Harlan', 'Jarrod Dixon') GROUP BY DisplayName;")
```
db.execute("SELECT DisplayName, MAX(Reputation) AS MaxReputation FROM users WHERE DisplayName IN ('Harlan', 'Jarrod Dixon') GROUP BY DisplayName;")
FINAL("WITH reps AS (SELECT DisplayName, MAX(Reputation) AS MaxReputation FROM users WHERE DisplayName IN ('Harlan', 'Jarrod Dixon') GROUP BY DisplayName) SELECT DisplayName FROM reps ORDER BY MaxReputation DESC LIMIT 1")

```python
db.execute("""
WITH reps AS (
  SELECT DisplayName, MAX(Reputation) AS MaxReputation
  FROM users
  WHERE DisplayName IN ('Harlan', 'Jarrod Dixon')
  GROUP BY DisplayName
)
SELECT DisplayName, MaxReputation
FROM reps
ORDER BY MaxReputation DESC, DisplayName
""")
```
db.execute("SELECT 1;")
FINAL("WITH reps AS (SELECT DisplayName, MAX(Reputation) AS MaxReputation FROM users WHERE DisplayName IN ('Harlan', 'Jarrod Dixon') GROUP BY DisplayName) SELECT DisplayName FROM reps ORDER BY MaxReputation DESC LIMIT 1;")

system prompt (679 chars)
You are a Text-to-SQL agent. Produce one read-only SQLite SELECT query that answers
the user question using only the provided question, evidence, schema, train examples,
and observable database results.

AVAILABLE TOOLS (inside ```python blocks):
  db.execute("SQL")
  db.sample_values("table", "column")

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence
     is needed.
  2. Execute the exact SQL you intend to submit and inspect its result.
  3. Submit plain text FINAL("YOUR SQL HERE") without a code block.
  4. Do not place tool code and FINAL in the same response.
  5. Do not use capabilities that are not explicitly listed.
task prompt (6066 chars)
QUESTION: Calculate the average view count of each post tagged as 'humor' and list the title and the comment of each post.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  tagged as  'humor' refers to tag = '<humor>'; comment of the post refers to Text; average view count = AVG(ViewCount)


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: codebase_community

TABLE: badges
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  UserId (INTEGER)  e.g. 5, 6
  Name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Teacher', 'Student'
  Date (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 19:39:07.0', '2010-07-19 19:39:08.0'
  FK: UserId → users.Id

TABLE: comments
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  PostId (INTEGER)  e.g. 3, 5
  Score (INTEGER)  e.g. 5, 0
  Text (TEXT)  e.g. 'Could be a poster child fo ...', 'Yes, R is nice- but WHY is ...'
  CreationDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 19:15:52.0', '2010-07-19 19:16:14.0'
  UserId (INTEGER)  e.g. 13, 37
  UserDisplayName (TEXT)  e.g. 'user28', 'Statprof'
  FK: UserId → users.Id
  FK: PostId → posts.Id

TABLE: postHistory
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  PostHistoryTypeId (INTEGER)  e.g. 2, 1
  PostId (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 2
  RevisionGUID (TEXT)  e.g. 'e58bf7fd-e60f-4c58-a6e4-dfc...', '18bf9150-f1cb-432d-b7b7-26d...'
  CreationDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 19:12:12.0', '2010-07-19 19:12:57.0'
  UserId (INTEGER)  e.g. 8, 24
  Text (TEXT)  e.g. 'How should I elicit prior d...', 'Eliciting priors from experts'
  Comment (TEXT)  e.g. '', 'edited tags'
  UserDisplayName (TEXT)  e.g. '', 'user28'
  FK: UserId → users.Id
  FK: PostId → posts.Id

TABLE: postLinks
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  CreationDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-21 14:47:33.0', '2010-07-23 16:30:41.0'
  PostId (INTEGER)  e.g. 395, 548
  RelatedPostId (INTEGER)  e.g. 173, 539
  LinkTypeId (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 3
  FK: RelatedPostId → posts.Id
  FK: PostId → posts.Id

TABLE: posts
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  PostTypeId (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 2
  AcceptedAnswerId (INTEGER)  e.g. 15, 59
  CreaionDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 19:12:12.0', '2010-07-19 19:12:57.0'
  Score (INTEGER)  e.g. 23, 22
  ViewCount (INTEGER)  e.g. 1278, 8198
  Body (TEXT)  e.g. '<p>How should I elicit prio...', '<p>In many different statis...'
  OwnerUserId (INTEGER)  e.g. 8, 24
  LasActivityDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-09-15 21:08:26.0', '2012-11-12 09:21:54.0'
  Title (TEXT)  e.g. 'Eliciting priors from experts', 'What is normality?'
  Tags (TEXT)  e.g. '<bayesian><prior><elicitation>', '<distributions><normality>'
  AnswerCount (INTEGER)  e.g. 5, 7
  CommentCount (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 4
  FavoriteCount (INTEGER)  e.g. 14, 8
  LastEditorUserId (INTEGER)  e.g. 88, 183
  LastEditDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-08-07 17:56:44.0', '2011-02-12 05:50:03.0'
  CommunityOwnedDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 19:13:28.0', '2010-07-19 19:14:43.0'
  ParentId (INTEGER)  e.g. 3, 7
  ClosedDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 20:19:46.0', '2010-08-05 13:06:12.0'
  OwnerDisplayName (TEXT)  e.g. 'user28', 'user209'
  LastEditorDisplayName (TEXT)  e.g. 'user28', 'user10525'
  FK: ParentId → posts.Id
  FK: OwnerUserId → users.Id
  FK: LastEditorUserId → users.Id

TABLE: tags
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  TagName (TEXT)  e.g. 'bayesian', 'prior'
  Count (INTEGER)  e.g. 1342, 168
  ExcerptPostId (INTEGER)  e.g. 20258, 62158
  WikiPostId (INTEGER)  e.g. 20257, 62157
  FK: ExcerptPostId → posts.Id

TABLE: users
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Reputation (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 101
  CreationDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 06:55:26.0', '2010-07-19 14:01:36.0'
  DisplayName (TEXT)  e.g. 'Community', 'Geoff Dalgas'
  LastAccessDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 06:55:26.0', '2013-11-12 22:07:23.0'
  WebsiteUrl (TEXT)  e.g. 'http://meta.stackexchange.com/', 'http://stackoverflow.com'
  Location (TEXT)  e.g. 'on the server farm', 'Corvallis, OR'
  AboutMe (TEXT)  e.g. "<p>Hi, I'm not really a per...", '<p>Developer on the StackOv...'
  Views (INTEGER)  e.g. 0, 25
  UpVotes (INTEGER)  e.g. 5007, 3
  DownVotes (INTEGER)  e.g. 1920, 0
  AccountId (INTEGER)  e.g. -1, 2
  Age (INTEGER)  e.g. 37, 35
  ProfileImageUrl (TEXT)  e.g. 'http://i.stack.imgur.com/d1...', 'http://i.stack.imgur.com/km...'

TABLE: votes
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  PostId (INTEGER)  e.g. 3, 2
  VoteTypeId (INTEGER)  e.g. 2, 5
  CreationDate (DATE)  e.g. '2010-07-19', '2010-07-20'
  UserId (INTEGER)  e.g. 58, 6
  BountyAmount (INTEGER)  e.g. 50, 25
  FK: UserId → users.Id
  FK: PostId → posts.Id

FINAL("WITH humor_posts AS ( SELECT Id, Title, AVG(ViewCount) AS AverageViewCount FROM posts WHERE instr(Tags, '<humor>') > 0 GROUP BY Id, Title ) SELECT hp.Title, c.Text AS Comment, hp.AverageViewCount FROM humor_posts hp JOIN comments c ON c.PostId = hp.Id;")

task prompt (6210 chars)
QUESTION: What is the percentage difference of student badges given during 2010 and 2011?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  student badges refers to badge's name = 'Student'; during 2010 refers to Year(Date) = 2010; during 2011 refers to Year(Date) = 2011; percentage difference = Subtract (Divide(Count(Name where Year(Date) = 2010), Count (Name)) *100, Divide(Count(Name where Year(Date) = 2011), Count(Name)) * 100)


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.execute("""
WITH student_badges AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM badges
  WHERE Name = 'Student'
)
SELECT
  (COUNT(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y', Date) = '2010' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*))
  - (COUNT(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y', Date) = '2011' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS percentage_difference
FROM student_badges;
""")
```
```python
db.execute("""
WITH student_badges AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM badges
  WHERE Name = 'Student'
)
SELECT
  (COUNT(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y', Date) = '2010' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*))
  - (COUNT(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y', Date) = '2011' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS percentage_difference
FROM student_badges;
""")
```
```python
db.execute("""
WITH student_badges AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM badges
  WHERE Name = 'Student'
)
SELECT
  (COUNT(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y', Date) = '2010' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*))
  - (COUNT(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y', Date) = '2011' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS percentage_difference
FROM student_badges;
""")
```
```python
db.execute("""
WITH student_badges AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM badges
  WHERE Name = 'Student'
)
SELECT
  (COUNT(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y', Date) = '2010' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*))
  - (COUNT(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y', Date) = '2011' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS percentage_difference
FROM student_badges;
""")
```
FINAL("WITH student_badges AS (SELECT Date FROM badges WHERE Name = 'Student') SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y', Date) = '2010' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) - (SUM(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y', Date) = '2011' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS percentage_difference FROM student_badges;")

system prompt (679 chars)
You are a Text-to-SQL agent. Produce one read-only SQLite SELECT query that answers

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence
     is needed.

  3. Submit plain text FINAL("YOUR SQL HERE") without a code block.
  4. Do not place tool code and FINAL in the same response.
  5. Do not use capabilities that are not explicitly listed.
task prompt (7101 chars)
QUESTION: What is the total number of non-chartered schools in the county of Los Angeles with a percent (%) of eligible free meals for grades 1 through 12 that is less than 0.18%?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  non-chartered schools refer to schools whose Charter = 0; K-12 means grades 1 through 12; percent of eligible free rate for K-12 = `Free Meal Count (K-12)` * 100 / `Enrollment (K-12)`


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: california_schools

TABLE: frpm

  Academic Year (TEXT)  e.g. '2014-2015'
  County Code (TEXT)  e.g. '01', '02'

  enroll12 (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 398, 62
  NumTstTakr (INTEGER) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 88, 17

  AvgScrMath (INTEGER)  e.g. 418, 546
  AvgScrWrite (INTEGER)  e.g. 417, 505

TABLE: schools

  ClosedDate (DATE)  e.g. '2015-07-31', '2015-06-30'

  CharterNum (TEXT)  e.g. '0728', '0811'
  FundingType (TEXT)  e.g. 'Directly funded', 'Locally funded'
  DOC (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. '00', '31'
  DOCType (TEXT) [NOT NULL]  e.g. 'County Office of Education ...', 'State Special Schools'

  SOCType (TEXT)  e.g. 'K-12 Schools (Public)', 'High Schools (Public)'

🤖 model turn:

```python
db.execute("""
SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt
FROM schools s
JOIN frpm f ON s.CDSCode = f.CDSCode
WHERE s.County = 'Los Angeles'
  AND s.Charter = 0
  AND (f.`Free Meal Count (K-12)` * 100.0 / f.`Enrollment (K-12)`) < 0.18;
""")
```
```python
db.execute("""
SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt
FROM schools s
JOIN frpm f ON s.CDSCode = f.CDSCode
WHERE s.County = 'Los Angeles'
  AND s.Charter = 0
  AND (f.`Free Meal Count (K-12)` * 100.0 / f.`Enrollment (K-12)`) < 0.18;
""")
```
```python
db.sample_values("schools", "County")
```
FINAL("SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_non_chartered_schools FROM frpm WHERE [County Name] = 'Los Angeles' AND [Charter School (Y/N)] = 0 AND (100.0 * [Free Meal Count (K-12)] / [Enrollment (K-12)]) < 0.18;")

task prompt (5987 chars)
QUESTION: Calculate the ratio of votes in 2010 and 2011.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  DIVIDE(COUNT(Id where YEAR(CreationDate) = 2010), COUNT(Id where YEAR(CreationDate) = 2011)) FROM votes;


TRAIN-ONLY SQL PATTERN LIBRARY:
Use these structural patterns as checks. They contain no evaluation schemas, values, or answers.
- Aggregate at the requested entity grain: For per-entity totals, averages, minima, or maxima, aggregate at the entity level first, then rank or limit the aggregated result. Do not rank individual detail rows when the question asks about an entity total.
- Apply filters before aggregate comparison: Keep the requested filter scope explicit before calculating an aggregate; check that the numerator, denominator, and grouping use the same intended population.
- Top or bottom result: For highest, lowest, earliest, latest, or top-k questions, verify the ORDER BY direction and apply LIMIT only after the requested grouping or filtering.
- Requested output columns: Return every requested field and only those fields, in the requested order. A multi-part question can require multiple SELECT expressions.
- Conditional scalar answer: For a literal yes/no question, return one conditional scalar value. For a request for rows or values, return those rows or values instead of a yes/no surrogate.
- List without duplicates: When a question asks for a list of entities across a one-to-many join, consider whether DISTINCT is required to preserve the requested output contract.
- Join path: When a question spans tables, verify each JOIN condition and select output columns from the table that owns the requested attribute.
- Explicit ranking: If the question asks for a rank number rather than sorted rows alone, use a window ranking expression and include the ranked metric when requested.
Schema:
DATABASE: codebase_community

TABLE: badges
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  UserId (INTEGER)  e.g. 5, 6
  Name (TEXT)  e.g. 'Teacher', 'Student'
  Date (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 19:39:07.0', '2010-07-19 19:39:08.0'
  FK: UserId → users.Id

TABLE: comments
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  PostId (INTEGER)  e.g. 3, 5
  Score (INTEGER)  e.g. 5, 0
  Text (TEXT)  e.g. 'Could be a poster child fo ...', 'Yes, R is nice- but WHY is ...'
  CreationDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 19:15:52.0', '2010-07-19 19:16:14.0'
  UserId (INTEGER)  e.g. 13, 37
  UserDisplayName (TEXT)  e.g. 'user28', 'Statprof'
  FK: UserId → users.Id
  FK: PostId → posts.Id

TABLE: postHistory
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  PostHistoryTypeId (INTEGER)  e.g. 2, 1
  PostId (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 2
  RevisionGUID (TEXT)  e.g. 'e58bf7fd-e60f-4c58-a6e4-dfc...', '18bf9150-f1cb-432d-b7b7-26d...'
  CreationDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 19:12:12.0', '2010-07-19 19:12:57.0'
  UserId (INTEGER)  e.g. 8, 24
  Text (TEXT)  e.g. 'How should I elicit prior d...', 'Eliciting priors from experts'
  Comment (TEXT)  e.g. '', 'edited tags'
  UserDisplayName (TEXT)  e.g. '', 'user28'
  FK: UserId → users.Id
  FK: PostId → posts.Id

TABLE: postLinks
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  CreationDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-21 14:47:33.0', '2010-07-23 16:30:41.0'
  PostId (INTEGER)  e.g. 395, 548
  RelatedPostId (INTEGER)  e.g. 173, 539
  LinkTypeId (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 3
  FK: RelatedPostId → posts.Id
  FK: PostId → posts.Id

TABLE: posts
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  PostTypeId (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 2
  AcceptedAnswerId (INTEGER)  e.g. 15, 59
  CreaionDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 19:12:12.0', '2010-07-19 19:12:57.0'
  Score (INTEGER)  e.g. 23, 22
  ViewCount (INTEGER)  e.g. 1278, 8198
  Body (TEXT)  e.g. '<p>How should I elicit prio...', '<p>In many different statis...'
  OwnerUserId (INTEGER)  e.g. 8, 24
  LasActivityDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-09-15 21:08:26.0', '2012-11-12 09:21:54.0'
  Title (TEXT)  e.g. 'Eliciting priors from experts', 'What is normality?'
  Tags (TEXT)  e.g. '<bayesian><prior><elicitation>', '<distributions><normality>'
  AnswerCount (INTEGER)  e.g. 5, 7
  CommentCount (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 4
  FavoriteCount (INTEGER)  e.g. 14, 8
  LastEditorUserId (INTEGER)  e.g. 88, 183
  LastEditDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-08-07 17:56:44.0', '2011-02-12 05:50:03.0'
  CommunityOwnedDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 19:13:28.0', '2010-07-19 19:14:43.0'
  ParentId (INTEGER)  e.g. 3, 7
  ClosedDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 20:19:46.0', '2010-08-05 13:06:12.0'
  OwnerDisplayName (TEXT)  e.g. 'user28', 'user209'
  LastEditorDisplayName (TEXT)  e.g. 'user28', 'user10525'
  FK: ParentId → posts.Id
  FK: OwnerUserId → users.Id
  FK: LastEditorUserId → users.Id

TABLE: tags
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  TagName (TEXT)  e.g. 'bayesian', 'prior'
  Count (INTEGER)  e.g. 1342, 168
  ExcerptPostId (INTEGER)  e.g. 20258, 62158
  WikiPostId (INTEGER)  e.g. 20257, 62157
  FK: ExcerptPostId → posts.Id

TABLE: users
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  Reputation (INTEGER)  e.g. 1, 101
  CreationDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 06:55:26.0', '2010-07-19 14:01:36.0'
  DisplayName (TEXT)  e.g. 'Community', 'Geoff Dalgas'
  LastAccessDate (DATETIME)  e.g. '2010-07-19 06:55:26.0', '2013-11-12 22:07:23.0'
  WebsiteUrl (TEXT)  e.g. 'http://meta.stackexchange.com/', 'http://stackoverflow.com'
  Location (TEXT)  e.g. 'on the server farm', 'Corvallis, OR'
  AboutMe (TEXT)  e.g. "<p>Hi, I'm not really a per...", '<p>Developer on the StackOv...'
  Views (INTEGER)  e.g. 0, 25
  UpVotes (INTEGER)  e.g. 5007, 3
  DownVotes (INTEGER)  e.g. 1920, 0
  AccountId (INTEGER)  e.g. -1, 2
  Age (INTEGER)  e.g. 37, 35
  ProfileImageUrl (TEXT)  e.g. 'http://i.stack.imgur.com/d1...', 'http://i.stack.imgur.com/km...'

TABLE: votes
  Id (INTEGER) [PK, NOT NULL]
  PostId (INTEGER)  e.g. 3, 2
  VoteTypeId (INTEGER)  e.g. 2, 5
  CreationDate (DATE)  e.g. '2010-07-19', '2010-07-20'
  UserId (INTEGER)  e.g. 58, 6
  BountyAmount (INTEGER)  e.g. 50, 25
  FK: UserId → users.Id
  FK: PostId → posts.Id

Follow the Hint above, explore the DB if needed, test your SQL, then FINAL("your sql").

system prompt (679 chars)
You are a Text-to-SQL agent. Produce one read-only SQLite SELECT query that answers

PROTOCOL:
  1. Inspect the supplied inputs and use only the listed tools when database evidence

  3. Submit plain text FINAL("YOUR SQL HERE") without a code block.
